text1: General purpose naval gun, loaded with high explosive rounds. Perfect for dealing with armored targets.
General purpose naval gun, loaded with high explosive rounds. Perfect for dealing with armored targets with its higher rate of fire.

A collaboration with an undisclosed partner, this variant of the Ivory weapon platform disperses bursts of cluster munitions, saturating the targeted area in ballistic shells, leaving no room to maneuver in areas directly at its fore. Meant to target pirate frigates and destroyers, its host ship, the Procrastria, is excellent as an escort vessel for patrols and convoys.
General purpose naval gun, it fires disruptive kinetic rounds designed to suppress enemy shields and weapons, but it doesn't do well against well armored targets.
Reliable naval gun, enhanced with additional barrels for greater firepower at the cost of more flux generation.

Standard medium caliber autocannon created with affordability and versatility in mind. It is a weaker and smaller version of the pre-existing Medlev series, designed to provide supporting fire. It's relatively low caliber for midsized autocannons makes it ideal for punching through frigates and destroyers, but struggles reaching vital subsystems on any larger ships.

Standard medium caliber autocannon created with affordability and versatility in mind. It is a weaker and smaller version of the pre-existing Medlev series, designed to provide supporting fire. While it does fire a larger caliber round, it still isn't enough to effectively harm larger ships. It is, however, significantly more deadly against smaller escort ships like destroyers and frigates.

Built using commonly available artillery components from the Yokata howitzer, the Shimata was originally designed to be used as a static ground defense weapon. The Auroran Navy quickly saw its potential as a naval weapon due to how effective and readily available it was.

The successful deployment of the smaller 155mm variant allowed the introduction of the next upgrade, the 300mm version. The caliber of choice may seem odd to some, but it is a necessary compromise in order to keep the weapon relatively cheap to produce and use. It would commonly be installed as part of ground defense system, overwatching the skies for any potential invasion or on reserve ships.
The standard general purpose autocannon, its rapid fire rate and excellent tracking capabilities make it a suitable defensive weapon.
Dual purpose twin-linked heavy autocannon, unlike the lighter variant. The Reina II is capable of tearing through shield with its kinetic rounds.
The standard general purpose autocannon, its high caliber kinetic rounds will suppress shields and knock out weapon systems.
The standard general purpose autocannon, its high caliber high explosive rounds will make a short work out of anything that's not bigger than a frigate.
The standard twin-linked flak autocannon, performs well against missiles and fighters but struggles against anything bigger than a corvette.
The enlarged version of standard Weiss autocannon. It boasts slightly better performance against missiles and fighters. Its extended barrels also slightly increase its range.
Weiss III is the largest of the reliable and thoroughly tested anti-air defense cannon series. It is equipped with experimental beehive shells that disperse incendiary shrapnel in a cone.
This small, standard railgun may not pack the biggest punch in the entirety of Auroran arsenal, but with enough of these no shield will stay standing.
Medium sized design of widely used railgun, a suitable weapon to suppress the enemy with constant barrage of kinetic rounds while staying at safe range.

The biggest of the Tona railgun series, this huge weapon will devastate enemy shields and temporarily disable their weapons. However, such firepower comes at the cost of a high amount of flux generated per shot, and slow fire rate.

The final evolution of the Tona railgun series before it turned into the eventual Phenex system, this huge weapon will devastate enemy shields and temporarily disable their weapons. However, such firepower comes at the cost of a high amount of flux generated per shot, and slow fire rate.

The final evolution of the Tona railgun series before it turned into the eventual Phenex system, this huge weapon will devastate enemy shields and temporarily disable their weapons. However, such firepower comes at the cost of a high amount of flux generated per shot, and slow fire rate.
Reliable Cyrexa modified with an additional barrel, for even faster rate of fire and even greater shield bursting potential.
The standard capital cannon. It masterfully mixes a decent rate of fire with some of the largest disruptive kinetic shells available to the UAF.
A single-barreled Cyrexa, modified to be as light as possible while preserving its mighty firepower. This gun is rumored to be mounted on some sort of experimental fighter…

An experimental weapon specifically designed for Solvernia. This triple barreled, high caliber railgun fires disruptive, high-penetration, rocket-boosted shells which make it highly effective against everything but the smallest, nimblest targets.

An experimental Close-In Weapon System armed with quadruple rotary autocannons, capable of intercepting incoming missiles and strikecraft with devastating barrage of high explosive fragmentation rounds.
The standard capital cannon. It fires high explosive rounds that are guaranteed to swiftly dispose of the enemy armor.
The standard capital cannon. It fires high explosive rounds that are guaranteed to swiftly dispose of the enemy armor at higher rate of fire.

The standard capital cannon installed on the Nonayen class Battlecruiser. It fires high explosive rounds that are guaranteed to swiftly dispose of the enemy. Its built-in nature allows greater rate of fire and lowered flux generation thanks to its unique revolver reloading system.
Dual rotary autocannons armed with high caliber, high explosive incendiary rounds, designed specifically to break down the enemy armor and melt them down with hail of lead.

A powerful air burst cannon capable of dealing with both small srikecraft and large capital ships, the Downstream excels at tearing through armor, hull and weapons alike. The closer ranges is a small price to pay to be able to control large swathes of open space.
The Axia series are the mass produced variants of a much bigger and experimental positron shock cannon project, the Garuda. This is the smallest of the three, typically installed on escort ships.
The Axia series are the mass produced variants of a much bigger and experimental positron shock cannon project, the Garuda. The medium version is ideal for cruisers and secondary for capital ships.
The Axia series are the mass produced variants of a much bigger and experimental positron shock cannon project, the Garuda. The largest version for capital ships or ones with the largest mount.

The Garuda 808 'Experimental Large Cannon' was the first of its kind. A mammoth undertaking that involved deep research and development into the mysteries of Persean advanced weaponry. It was developed alongside the Novaeria project and was meant to be installed on the flagship as her main armament. The raw volatile energy it produced continuously when charging proved to be too dangerous to store, forcing it to vent by channeling them into the barrel, thus creating high intensity ray of beam. Once fully charged, it will unleash powerful antielectron projectiles.

It was developed alongside the Novaeria project and was meant to be installed on the flagship as her main armament. The raw volatile energy it produced continuously when charging proved to be too dangerous to store, forcing it to vent by channeling them into the barrel, thus creating high intensity ray of beam. Once fully charged, it will unleash powerful antielectron projectiles.

The standard capital cannon that can be found on artillery light cruisers, it doesn't strike hard or deep like its counterparts but can still be viable once the enemy's hull is vulnerable to claw into.

The 'Decorative' is a low cost, low caliber auxillary weapon system mainly used for point defense. It is often underestimated by friendly and foe alike but some may find it strategically viable when used correctly.

Merupaka I is a heavy dual purpose autocannon intended to be used by the Automata forces. It fires energy infused rounds in rapid burst which makes it a jack-of-all-trades in general, but also master of none as it doesn't specialize in anything in specific.

Merupaka II is a heavy dual purpose autocannon intended to be used by the Automata forces. It fires energy infused rounds in rapid burst which makes it a jack-of-all-trades in general, but also master of none as it doesn't specialize in anything in specific.

The Edge series are the first of its kind to ever enter mass production under request of the Automata Division. Each gun from Edge series is equipped with an experimental mechanism that allows them to convert the heat generated by the ship's reactor into flux-infused rounds, dealing a higher amount of disruptive damage while also helping with the flux ventilation, granted it's being used under the right circumstances. This smallest variant fires in bursts.

The Edge series are the first of its kind to ever enter mass production under request of the Automata Division. Each gun from Edge series is equipped with an experimental mechanism that allows them to convert the heat generated by the ship's reactor into flux-infused rounds, dealing a higher amount of disruptive damage while also helping with the flux ventilation, granted it's being used under the right circumstances. This medium variant fires continuously.

The Edge series are the first of its kind to ever enter mass production under request of the Automata Division. Each gun from Edge series is equipped with an experimental mechanism that allows them to convert the heat generated by the ship's reactor into flux-infused rounds, dealing a higher amount of disruptive damage while also helping with the flux ventilation, granted it's being used under the right circumstances. This large variant fires in bursts.

The Edge series are the first of its kind to ever enter mass production under request of the Automata Division. Each gun from Edge series is equipped with an experimental mechanism that allows them to convert the heat generated by the ship's reactor into flux-infused shells, dealing a higher amount of disruptive damage while also helping with the flux ventilation, granted it's being used under the right circumstances. This unique super capital variant fires in bursts of two rounds

Catun railgun series are an energy-conversion of the pre-existing Tona railguns. Compared to Tona it lacks the trademark penetration capability since the shell was changed to be fitted with a lighter, energy-infused warhead. Smaller, more advanced munitions allow it to fire slightly faster than the predecessor and to destroy targets quicker, as long as the shell makes it through enemy armor.

Catun railgun series are an energy-conversion of the pre-existing Tona railguns. Compared to Tona it lacks the trademark penetration capability since the shell was changed to be fitted with a lighter, energy-infused warhead. Smaller, more advanced munitions allow it to fire slightly faster than the predecessor and to destroy targets quicker, as long as the shell makes it through enemy armor.

Kanshee is an alternative choice for the Automata forces. It sacrificed rate of fire in return for a linked salvo of devasting highly concentrated energy infused high explosive shells capable of tearing through armor or shield alike.

Kanshee is an alternative choice for the Automata forces. It sacrificed rate of fire in return for a linked salvo of devasting highly concentrated energy infused high explosive shells capable of tearing through armor or shield alike. An additional barrel was installed in this version for extra firepower.

Next generation upgrade to the existing Cyrexa weapon system, its improved fire control and recoil dampener systems allow it to fire slightly faster than its predecessor while also enjoying the brand new energy-infused shells at the cost of higher flux generation.

An alternative to the Cyrexa II weapon system, this one boasts higher caliber and generates greater amount of flux for a bigger punch and higher projectile speed. Its improved fire control and recoil dampener systems allow it to fire slightly faster than its predecessor while also enjoying the brand new energy-infused shells.

Precisia was the Federation's introduction into energy weaponry. It fires in two bursts, and uses its advanced targeting systems to strike the weak spots of the enemy ships with unparalleled accuracy.

The next step in the Triyia series of point defense, the Enyria boasts the brand-new energy tech developed by the Automata Expeditionary Division. The Enyria fires in short bursts in order to protect the weapon 
both from overheating and potentially frying itself from its own EMP generator.
While technically considered a weapon under UAF law, to the point it requires interfacing with fire control systems, the Eyeseau Radar is not capable of damaging enemy ships. It is a support tool.
Rapid-firing missile system designed to overwhelm the enemy with pure numbers. It also comes with decent tracking capabilities which allows it to intercept incoming strikecraft as well.
Rapid-firing missile system designed to overwhelm the enemy with pure numbers. It also comes with decent tracking capabilities which allows it to intercept incoming strikecraft as well.
Rapid-firing missile system designed to overwhelm the enemy with pure numbers. It also comes with decent tracking capabilities which allows it to intercept incoming strikecraft as well.
Standard anti-ship missile, packed with high explosive filler to penetrate through armored targets.
Standard anti-ship missile, packed with high explosive filler to penetrate through armored targets.
Standard anti-ship missile, packed with high explosive filler to penetrate through enemy armor. This one is mounted on a self-reloading pod that allows for continuous rapid fire.

Large unguided torpedo armed with a fragmentation flechette warhead. While it's immensely powerful as a tool to finish off slow or immobilized ships with exposed hulls thanks to its deadly fragmentation payload, it struggles against armor and shields. This version is installed on a self-reloading turret.

Large unguided torpedo armed with a fragmentation flechette warhead. While it's immensely powerful as a tool to finish off slow or immobilized ships with exposed hulls thanks to its deadly fragmentation payload, it struggles against armor and shields.

Experimental, small "anti-everything" cruise missile, based on the infamous Semibreve. It quickly gained popularity among ship captains that favor much more destructive nuclear weaponry over conventional anti-ship torpedoes. Its excellent guidance together with the fact that it can be installed on any ship only speak further in its favor.

Experimental, small "anti-everything" cruise missile, based on the infamous Semibreve. It quickly gained popularity among ship captains that favor much more destructive nuclear weaponry over conventional anti-ship torpedoes. Its excellent guidance together with the fact that it can be installed on any ship only speak further in its favor. This one comes in a pod capable of forging new missiles.

The dreaded “last-resort” weapon of the Auroran forces. Despite its destructive power resembling nuclear warheads of ancient civilizations, this WMD utilizes a warhead composed of matter called Shinonian Particles. These particles are extremely unstable, and are constantly attempting to rip apart reality around it. The result is that there is little need to make a proper primer for the warhead. Instead, the contact trigger actuates a hammer that cracks the containment field, letting the highly compressed particles release the torrent of energy contained within. Supposedly composed of negative emotion compressed to the point of physical manifestation, the dire straits of the expeditionary fleet and the vitriol directed towards them by sector natives ensures that raw material for more warheads is never in short supply.

The dreaded “last-resort” weapon of the Auroran forces, with extra spice as it is now guided. Despite its destructive power resembling nuclear warheads of ancient civilizations, this WMD utilizes a warhead composed of matter called Shinonian Particles. Missile system firing guided high explosive missiles in volleys of three. The missile's role are to saturate and overcome enemy defenses with pure numbers.
Missile system firing guided high explosive missiles in volleys up to twelve. The missile's role are to saturate and overcome enemy defenses with pure numbers.
Standard anti-ship torpedo, deadly against ships and stations. It also comes with a capable tracking system.
Standard anti-ship torpedo, deadly against ships and stations. It also comes with a capable tracking system and miniaturized shield to protect the payload from enemy point defense.
Standard anti-ship torpedo, deadly against ships and stations. It also comes with a capable tracking system. This one comes in pod capable of reloading new missiles.

Standard anti-ship torpedo, deadly against ships and stations. It also comes with a capable tracking system. This one comes in a rack capable of reloading new missiles and fires in salvo of four missiles.

Disruptive anti-ship missile, equipped with excellent avionics and disruptive explosive filler to encircle the enemy and disable their engines. This one is capable of launching up to five missiles in salvo.
Disruptive anti-ship missile, equipped with excellent avionics and disruptive explosive filler to encircle the enemy and disable their engines. This one is installed on a self-reloading pod.
Disruptive anti-ship missile, equipped with excellent avionics and disruptive explosive filler to encircle the enemy and disable their engines.
Lightweight unguided cluster torpedo. It rapidly accelerates forward and delivers a powerful blow to enemy shields.
Lightweight unguided cluster torpedo with slightly heavier payload. It rapidly accelerates forward and delivers a powerful blow to enemy shields.
Lightweight unguided cluster torpedo with slightly heavier payload. It rapidly accelerates forward and delivers a powerful blow to enemy shields.

Experimental torpedo armed with high explosive warheads. It will continue to accelerate towards the target until it reaches the optimal distance before dispersing into multiple guided cluster munitions.

Experimental torpedo armed with high explosive warheads. It will continue to accelerate towards the target until it reaches the optimal distance before dispersing into multiple guided cluster munitions.

Experimental torpedo armed with kinetic warheads. It will continue to accelerate towards the target until it reaches the optimal distance before dispersing into multiple unguided cluster munitions.
Experimental torpedo armed with kinetic warheads. It will continue to accelerate towards the target until it reaches the optimal distance before dispersing into multiple unguided cluster munitions.
Experimental torpedo armed with kinetic warheads. It will continue to accelerate towards the target until it reaches the optimal distance before dispersing into multiple unguided cluster munitions.

Multi-launch rocket system designed for shield suppression. It fires a stream of unguided, shield breaking rockets fitted with small explosive filler that allows them to deal acceptable damage against armor should the shields fall.

Multi-launch rocket system designed for shield suppression. It fires a stream of unguided, shield breaking rockets fitted with small explosive filler that allows them to deal acceptable damage against armor should the shields fall. This one is simply an enlarged version with a greater number of missiles.
Pod loaded with flares and chaffs designed to distract enemy fighters and missiles, giving the captain of the ship more time to react to incoming threats.

Point defense weapon armed with multiple micro-missiles designed as a last line of defense against enemy fighters and missiles. It was accepted into service after the navy noticed how effective it was at protecting the ground forces' armored vehicles against incoming missile attacks.As effective as it is, it requires a brief moment to recalibrate itself after every shot, and as such it should not be used as a main point defense weapon.
Standard anti-air missile system, capable of effectively intercepting incoming threats and annihilating strikecraft thanks to its advanced avionics and excellent tracking capabilities.
Standard anti-air missile system, capable of effectively intercepting incoming threats and annihilating strikecraft thanks to its advanced avionics and excellent tracking capabilities.
Standard anti-air missile system, capable of effectively intercepting incoming threats and annihilating strikecraft thanks to its advanced avionics and excellent tracking capabilities.

Experimental nuclear anti-air missile system, capable of effectively intercepting incoming threats and annihilating strikecraft thanks to its advanced avionics and excellent tracking capabilities. It's also armed with a small tactical nuclear warhead to deal with grouped missiles and fighters.
'Sakura' is a multi launch missile system that launches long range guided micro missiles. Relying on mass saturation attack to overwhelm enemy missile defense.

'Sakura' is a multi launch missile system that launches long range guided micro missiles. Relying on mass saturation attack to overwhelm enemy missile defense. This variant was formed into a group of vertical launch tubes on Solvernia with higher amount of missiles.

'Sakura' is a multi launch missile system that launches long range guided micro missiles. Relying on mass saturation attack to overwhelm enemy missile defense. The catapult pod is a limited production version that allows installation on ships that can support such a massive weapon.

An experimental two-staged anti-ship cruise missile, it will try to flank the enemy from the side during the first stage and then separate its high explosive warhead that will rapidly accelerate towards the target.
Standard rotary rapid-fire autocannon, equipped with high explosive incendiary rounds that are popular among auroran fighters.
Standard rotary rapid-fire autocannon, equipped with high explosive disruptive rounds to stun and then neutralize the enemy.
Miniaturized version of the Triyia CIWS, excels in providing anti-air support and suppressing enemy fighters.
Standard rotary rapid-fire autocannon, upscaled to higher caliber. Proven to be very effective against all kinds of targets.
Rotary rapid-fire weapon loaded with a mix of experimental energy-infused rounds and conventional high explosive incendiary ammunition.

An upgrade to the rotary rapid-firing EMG autocannon, featuring higher caliber and newly introduced energy rounds, during its so far short service time it already proven itself to be very effective against all kinds of targets.
Low caliber railgun loaded with kinetic rounds based on the shipborne 'Tona' for strikecraft uses.
Low caliber railgun loaded with kinetic rounds based on the shipborne 'Tona' for strikecraft uses.
Medium caliber standard turret armed with kinetic rounds, capable of rapid firing.
A legacy variant of the infamous shipborne 'Penulisa' dual chaingun, it fires 40mm high explosive rounds instead of 50mm in order to save up space.
The defensive turret armed with proximity fused flak shells, capable of firing at rapid pace. It recently entered service on board the air defense gunship and the super heavy bomber.
Heavy caliber autocannon loaded with high explosive rounds typically found on dedicated strikecraft.
Heavy caliber autocannon loaded with high explosive rounds typically found on dedicated strikecraft.
Triple barreled heavy caliber autocannon turret loaded with high explosive rounds dedicated for the assault gunship. Its a miniaturized version of the shipborne 'Ivory Rapid' weapon system.

The Yokata howitzer originated from land-based howitzer designs before being adapted into ground defense role. Its effectiveness in that role and emergency wartime measures resulted in its improvisation as naval gun later on.

The 'High Velocity Anti-Ship Rocket' is a large unguided high explosive rocket typically used on a multirole spacecraft. Allowing the user to create a small opening through the armor to deal greater damage, typically in coordination with friendly wings.

The 'High Velocity Anti-Ship Rocket' is a unguided high explosive rocket typically used on a multirole spacecraft. This is a much lighter fragmentation sub-variant launch from a rocket cylinder pod with the purpose of overwhelming enemy air defenses with a saturation attack.

The 'High Velocity Anti-Ship Rocket' is a unguided high explosive rocket typically used on a multirole spacecraft. This is a much lighter kinetic piercing sub-variant launch from a rocket cylinder pod with the purpose of overwhelming enemy air defenses with a saturation attack.

The 'High Velocity Anti-Ship Rocket' is a large unguided high explosive rocket typically used on a multirole spacecraft. Allowing the user to create a small opening through the armor to deal greater damage, typically in coordination with friendly wings.

The 'High Velocity Anti-Ship Rocket' is a high capacity rocket pod that cold launches a salvo of unguided rockets. Once fully discharged, It will retract into the ship and reload fully by swapping with a new pod. The rocket may also re-align and propel itself towards the enemy based on the data provided by data-link from the ship.

The 'Vocal Aerial Standard Missile' is a guided micro missile based on the shipborne version. Much of its high explosive payload was sacrificed to make room for the high performance thrusters and advanced guidance system.

The 'Advanced Multi-purpose Space-to-Space Missile' is a variant of ship-based Chorus missile system for strikecraft uses. Capable of intercepting incoming threats and neutralizing enemy strikecraft. It relies on its proximity fuse to know when to detonate and deal fragmentation damage.

The 'Advanced Multi-purpose Space-to-Space Missile' is a variant of ship-based Chorus missile system for strikecraft uses. Capable of intercepting incoming threats and neutralizing enemy strikecraft. It relies on its proximity fuse to know when to detonate and deal fragmentation damage.

The 'Advanced Multi-purpose Space-to-Space Missile' is a variant of ship-based Chorus missile system for strikecraft uses. Capable of intercepting incoming threats and neutralizing enemy strikecraft. It relies on its proximity fuse to know when to detonate and deal fragmentation damage.

The 'Advanced Multi-purpose Space-to-Space Missile Experimental' is a sub-variant of ship-based Chorus missile system for strikecraft uses. Relying on proximity fuse to know when to detonate it's low yield tactical nuclear warhead, making ideal against swarms of enemy fighters or to harass enemy ships.

The 'Advanced Multi-purpose Space-to-Space Missile Experimental' is a sub-variant of ship-based Chorus missile system for strikecraft uses. Relying on proximity fuse to know when to detonate it's low yield tactical nuclear warhead, making ideal against swarms of enemy fighters or to harass enemy ships.

Exoscar is the Federation's standard issue anti-ship missile commonly used by strikecraft and on board ships. Although reasonably guided for a torpedo class, it doesn't maneuver well due to the heavy weight displacement by the on-board guidance system and the high explosive warhead.

Exoscar is the Federation's standard issue anti-ship missile commonly used by strikecraft and on board ships. Although reasonably guided for a torpedo class, it doesn't maneuver well due to the heavy weight displacement by both the on-board guidance system and the enhanced high explosive warhead.

Exoscar is the Federation's standard issue anti-ship missile commonly used by strikecraft and on board ships. Although reasonably guided for a torpedo class, it doesn't maneuver well due to the heavy weight displacement by both the on-board guidance system and the experimental energy infused explosive warhead.

The 'Kinetic Cluster Medium Range' is one of twin sub variants of the experimental evolution of the standard missile, capable of dispersing into multiple kinetic sub-missiles upon reaching ideal distance towards the enemy.

The 'High Explosive Cluster Medium Range' is one of two sub variants of the experimental evolution of the standard missile, capable of dispersing into multiple high explosive sub-missiles upon reaching ideal distance towards the enemy.

The 'Anti-Ship Medium Range' standard guided missile is strikecraft variant of the ship-based Clemente missile that relies on information provided by data-link from the spacecraft for guidance. Once target acquired, it becomes self-guided and continue to pursue until impact.

The 'Anti-Ship Medium Range Class II' is the next evolution of the standard guided missile found on smaller spacecrafts. This particular type is twice as large, guided and carries heavier payload of fragmentation warhead.

The Minibreve is a legendary guided cruise missile armed with a tactical nuclear warhead that can completely tear through the enemy defenses and annihilate them with a single blow. Proves to be devastating when used in numbers or coordination with other friendly wings.

The 'Advanced Weapon Package' is a summarized acronym for a new armored ballistic missile type made for the Rafflesya. Allowing the super-heavy bomber to release its payload from far greater range without the need to expose itself to enemy fire.

The 'Advanced Weapon Package' is a summarized acronym for a new armored ballistic missile type made for the Rafflesya. Allowing the super-heavy bomber to release its payload from far greater range without the need to expose itself to enemy fire.

A torpedo launcher capable of firing the experimental Lion's Roar nuclear torpedo. Its unique delayed fuse armored warhead design makes it a remarkably sturdy and fast torpedo capable of bouncing into the target's formation before exploding within in two separate phases, implosion and explosion.

There are no Auroran military personnel who do not believe the Nakiha is an exceptional design. Entering its fourth generation, Nakiha-class frigates form the backbone of the Auroran naval forces, largely because of its capability to perform multiple roles, whether that be screening for larger ships, or forming fast response patrols. Some brave captains have even destroyed much larger capital ships by taking advantage of its maneuverability and torpedo armaments.

Its ability to enter and leave, or even land and take off from within a planet’s atmosphere without assistance from mass driver runways has also played into the ship’s longevity in the Auroran Navy. This functionality played a crucial role during the Battle for Aurora, in which they were simultaneously deployed in conflicts from high orbit to stratosphere and every altitude in between. This nimble and versatile warship should never be underestimated by its foe, as it may hold a couple of reserve surprises specifically for the oblivious.

This ship has been retrofitted and heavily modified to meet the specifications required by the Auroran Automata Expeditionary Division. It's equipped with the latest avionics, prototype upgrades and additional external missile racks are installed to the side of the engines, armed with Clemente anti-ship missiles. Allowing it to outperform its predecessors while also a boasting lower sensor profile, thanks to the stealth technology behind its durable and unique AED coating.

There are no Auroran military personnel who do not believe the Nakiha is an exceptional design. Entering its fourth generation, Nakiha-class frigates are the backbone of the Auroran naval forces, largely because of its capability to enter and leave, or even land and take off from terran-sized planets without assistance from mass driver runways. This functionality played a crucial role in the defense of the homeworld during the Battle for Aurora, in which they were simultaneously deployed in conflicts from high orbit to stratosphere and every altitude in between. This nimble and versatile warship should never be underestimated by its foe, as it may hold a couple of reserve surprises specifically for the oblivious.

Additional armor plates are installed on critical part of the ship to improve its survivability.

This ship was given up-to-date military-grade upgrades and a unique camouflage pattern belonging to the Auroran Royal Navy's November Fleet, personally led by the Aeria Charlotte herself. Additional armor plating and durable protective coating were installed to enhance the survivability of this ship; albeit at the cost of slightly lowered maneuverability and speed due to additional weight.

Seiichi is a class of destroyer designed and built by the former Ceyaian Space Engineering Bureau No. 196, now merged into a multi-national coalition of manufacturing companies under the leadership of a corporation known as Armok Industries and Manufacturing. For a long time, they served as the workhorse of the Auroran Navy, all the way up to the Battle of Aurora before being ultimately phased out by the jack-of-all-trades, the Nakiha class Frigate Leader.

With the outbreak of the war, the ship was hastily brought back into service in order to fill in the desperately needed role as a heavy hitter - something the first generation frigates were not capable of due to their lightweight design. Its heavy armor and armament proved to be invaluable as they allowed her to engage and stay in the fight much longer alongside the battlecruisers. By the time the navy gathered enough experience to introduce second generation ships, however, the outdated Seiichi had begun to show its age and has been relegated to support roles to this day.

This ship was given up-to-date military-grade upgrades and a unique camouflage pattern belonging to the Auroran Royal Navy's November Fleet which is led personally by the Queen of Yuria herself. Additional armor plating and durable protective coating were installed to enhance the survivability of this ship; albeit at the cost of slightly lowered maneuverability and speed due to additional weight.

Seiichi is a class of destroyer designed and built by the former Ceyaian Space Engineering Bureau No. 196, now merged into a multi-national coalition of manufacturing companies under the leadership of a corporation known as Armok Industries and Manufacturing. For a long time, they served as the workhorse of the Auroran Navy, all the way up to the Battle of Aurora before being ultimately phased out by the jack-of-all-trades, the Nakiha class Frigate Leader.

In order to save time and funds on the development of a new heavy destroyer, AIM opted to update the Seiichi design. The new Seiichi-Kai was much larger than the original, barely qualifying as a destroyer. In addition to heavier armor and firepower, it also received a unique command suite capable of communicating with both modern Auroran ships and the more outdated systems found on the older Seiichi models. As a result, a Kai is often deployed to act as a command ship for Seiichi squadrons, improving their efficiency on the battlefield, despite their age.

This ship was given up-to-date military-grade upgrades and a unique camouflage pattern belonging to the Auroran Royal Navy's November Fleet which is led personally by the Queen of Yuria herself. Additional armor plating and durable protective coating were installed to enhance the survivability of this ship; albeit at the cost of slightly lowered maneuverability and speed due to additional weight.

While the introduction of the ever-popular Nakiha-class Frigate Leaders have carried the Auroran Navy since their introduction, their time in the Persean Sector has made it obvious to the Auroran Navy that destroyers still have a place even in space warfare, a class that has been largely ignored in favor of the maneuverability of frigates or the heavier firepower of cruisers and capitals. This has left a gap in the Auroran lineup that helps bring the two sides together, light enough to maintain its maneuverability, but heavy enough to bring cruisers to heel. The Vaharagiri was their answer to a popular demand for a hybrid support ship.

She is actually a major modification of another prototype destroyer, the Super Nakiha as she borrows several features from her which resulted in more power and increased operating range. The main difference between the Vaharagiri and the rest of the upcoming destroyer series are her main weapon mounts which can support all kinds of weaponry as a future-proof design. However, such advanced features consumed most of the internal space and added a ton of weight upon the hull. This was made possible only due to major compromises in terms of armor protection and overall structural integrity which should be manageable, so long as its captain makes good use of its shields.

This ship has been retrofitted and heavily modified to meet the specifications required by the Auroran Automata Expeditionary Division. It's equipped with the latest avionics and prototype upgrades, allowing it to outperform its predecessors while also a boasting lower sensor profile, thanks to the stealth technology behind its durable and unique AED coating.

Immediately after combat deployments of the recently commissioned Vaharagiri-class destroyers, reports and feedback regarding her performance convinced the Auroran Navy to revise and improve the design wherever possible. Additionally, the Auroran Navy was still unsure how to utilize these new ships, having spent so long relying on frigates and cruisers. Whilst working on fixes, the Navy decided that a revised design could fill the role of a destroyer leader instead, in order to improve coordination among the fleet to make up for the inexperience. Thus, after a remarkably short development cycle, the upgraded Vachasya-class destroyer soon enters service as a true multirole destroyer leader and serves alongside the smaller Vaharagiri-class destroyers.

The Vachasya-class destroyer features a number of revisions and improvements over the original Vaharagiri-class destroyer, being larger in size and sporting a revised layout, including an improved command suite for coordinating operations with accompanying Vahagiris. One of the key lessons learnt is that Vaharagiri’s radiators and engine blocks are particularly vulnerable during combat due to their placements at the wing sections. To address this vulnerability, as well as enhancing the reactor’s efficiency, a majority of Vachasya’s core ship systems like reactor and flux grids are moved to the centerline, thus freeing up space for extra mounts and thrusters to compensate. More importantly is the enlarged hull, which allows for improvement in flux management and addressing some of the structural problems. Thus, Vasachya’s hull is more space efficient, allowing for the mounting of a better shield generator and additional weaponry, while still being able to provide reliable coordination efforts to her assigned escorts. The improvements, whilst seemingly minor, turned the design into a reliable multirole destroyer, whether the task be escorting, screening or hunting. 

This ship has been retrofitted and heavily modified to meet the specifications required by the Auroran Automata Expeditionary Division. It's equipped with the latest avionics and prototype upgrades, allowing it to outperform its predecessors while also a boasting lower sensor profile, thanks to the stealth technology behind its durable and unique AED coating.

There are no Auroran military personnel who do not believe the Nakiha is an exceptional design. Entering its fourth generation, Nakiha-class frigates are the backbone of the Auroran naval forces, largely because of its capability to enter and leave, or even land and take off from terran-sized planets without assistance from mass driver runways. This functionality played a crucial role in the defense of the homeworld during the Battle for Aurora, in which they were simultaneously deployed in conflicts from high orbit to stratosphere and every altitude in between. This nimble and versatile warship should never be underestimated by its foe, as it may hold a couple of reserve surprises specifically for the oblivious.

The latest variant of the venerable Nakiha class frigate, the Super Nakiha was the result of collaboration between a frigate ace squadron and the primary naval design bureau. Its increased size allows more room for advanced internal upgrades, such as high-performance cruiser-grade engines supported by an even more efficient power plant, giving it a far greater thrust to weight ratio than a ship its size ought to have. The expenses for such upgrades, however, have limited production, and while a dozen were planned to be produced, only three made it out of the prototype stage thus far. The ship class was rumored to be cursed when the first prototype destroyer was nearly annihilated by a freak friendly fire accident caused by another during live fire trials. No details regarding the incident ever surfaced as it was quickly covered up by the navy but regardless, a legend was born because of it.

This ship was given up-to-date military-grade upgrades and a unique camouflage pattern belonging to the Auroran Royal Navy's November Fleet, personally led by the Aeria Charlotte herself. Additional armor plating and durable protective coating were installed to enhance the survivability of this ship; albeit at the cost of slightly lowered maneuverability and speed due to additional weight.

The Straffia is one of the United Aurora Federation’s first forays into automated warfare. Built by Armok Industries and Manufacturing for deep space reconnaissance, it was these dutiful drone ships that first encountered the Federation’s new adversaries at the gate. Only armed for self defence, they were quickly dispatched, but not before they sent back crucial information: The Federation was not alone in space.

Following the Battle of Aurora and subsequent expedition into the Persean Sector, the Federation was in dire need of new information regarding their surroundings. With a scant few resources to spare, AIM opted to re-introduce the Straffia as a picket ship. These new Straffias were altered from their pre-expedition counterparts, being somewhat larger to accommodate new missile batteries for taking on larger vessels. This has come at a cost: self-maintenance equipment was sacrificed, dramatically lowering its operational range away from a friendly fleet, and  it has been known to explode spectacularly when shot due to the new missile magazines occupying a disproportionally large amount of space on board.

The Moraffia is an unconventional, lightly armed warship designed by an independent group of captains and accepted into production by the Armok Industries and Manufacturing. Making it the Federation’s second official entry into the automated warfare doctrine. Whilst there were an excellent variety of choices among the existing ship roster, none of them would satisfy the demands of a ship that can carry supplies with extended range for a lengthy expedition. This led to the creation of Moraffia to serve as an alternative choice as an AWACS platform of a light carrier squadron in place of the much larger and more expensive Eyeseau, or serve as the lead escort of a logistic fleet. The Moraffia is also able to support the fleet by deploying long range drones mounting the reliable Tona-I railgun, allowing it to pressure the shields of much larger vessels at a safe distance.

While the Straffia proved to be excellent scouts and pickets, the Federation still struggled to coordinate large groups of them. The Moraffia as a result became a sort of mothership. Commands are given to the Moraffia, which then calculates and assigns its subordinate Straffias accordingly. Additionally, these capabilities were able to be shared with manned vessels as well, allowing the Moraffia to pair with a Minoria, granting it similar command and control capabilities to more capable carriers. While the scouting role has largely been ceded to the Nakihas following the colonization of Aurora, Moraffias are still deployed to organize and support Straffia patrols on the fringes of Auroran territories.

The Moraffia is an unconventional, lightly armed warship designed by an independent group of captains and accepted into production by the Armok Industries and Manufacturing. Making it the Federation’s second official entry into the automated warfare doctrine. Whilst there were an excellent variety of choices among the existing ship roster, none of them would satisfy the demands of a ship that can carry supplies with extended range for a lengthy expedition. This led to the creation of Moraffia to serve as an alternative choice as an AWACS platform of a light carrier squadron in place of the much larger and more expensive Eyeseau, or serve as the lead escort of a logistic fleet. The Moraffia is also able to support the fleet by deploying long range drones mounting the reliable Tona-I railgun, allowing it to pressure the shields of much larger vessels at a safe distance.

Moraffia II on the other hand is a new limited production variant, meant to compete against the Scinaut class Science Ship with several notable advantages. In order to be more capable of defending itself on lone scouting missions, its railgun drones have been traded for a more general purpose combat drone. Larger and more armored, these drones require additional space for storage and manufacturing. As a result, some of the redundancies and non-vital components were taken off the ship to maintain the ship’s relatively small profile.

When designing their first frigate, there was a heated debate on whether she should be armed with ballistic weaponry, or missile hardpoints. In a bizarre twist, the Auroran Navy actually adopted both. The ballistic variant would become the famous Nakiha-series of frigates. The design for the missile variant would be shelved… until the Battle of Aurora witnessed the birth of the Purcellyra series of missile battlecruisers. With missile weaponry seeing viable use in warfare, the Auroran Navy sought to scale down the Purcellyra into a smaller, but still just as deadly package. Ship designers returned to the abandoned missile frigate, and within a few months, the Novayu was born.

She shares more in common with the Nakiha than just her role. The Novayu also has a great deal of flexibility. She performs a wide variety of roles, ranging from assisting from long range with powerful anti-shield missiles such as the Amazoch kinetic torpedo, closing to deliver swift finishing blows against crippled targets with the Exoscar anti-ship torpedo, or providing fighter screening for the squadron with Chorus anti-air batteries. It is not uncommon for multiple variants of Novayu to cover multiple positions within the formation.

The Novayu is not deployed as the core of a frigate squadron like the Nakiha. Realizing that missiles cannot perform as consistently as ballistic weaponry, the Novayu has instead been deployed in support of her sister ships. As a result, she tends to be rarer than the Nakiha, but is nonetheless invaluable to the Federation’s fast response squadrons.

“Fire the missiles! Fire them again! Fire them ALL!” - Commander Mungsk

With the advent of larger ships becoming atmosphere capable, such as the Machi-series landing ships or the ever-present Nakihas, the need for shuttles that can transition from atmosphere into high orbit has dropped considerably. However, the old but proven Perwira shuttle design has proven popular among older Auroran naval officers, and has since seen a resurgence strong enough to see these shuttles put back into service. 

Originally designed to transport personnel to and from planetary installations, its armament was minimal, largely used to defend itself against would-be assailants within atmospheric limits. The shuttle, however, ended up seeing use in unintentional ways. The Perwira has the ability to break free of planetary atmosphere and gravitational fields akin to a single-stage rocket, boasting unrivaled speed in high orbit thanks to its Melody Micro Drive, and enabling it to outpace the ships made to carry it while in conventional space. This, coupled with “upgraded” weapons systems in the form of dual missile hardpoints, have had the Perwira being pressed into the role of a pseudo-fighter, earning it the nickname: “The Warrior”. Several commanders have reported success using it to dogfight alongside the only marginally slower Rama-class Corvettes and Nakihas, and the shuttle continues to see use amongst daredevils despite claims of redundancy by more sane officers.

This ship has been retrofitted and heavily modified to meet the specifications required by the Auroran Automata Expeditionary Division. It's equipped with the latest avionics and prototype upgrades, allowing it to outperform its predecessors while also a boasting lower sensor profile, thanks to the stealth technology behind its durable and unique AED coating.

The Reisen class Battleship is one of the primary capital warships in use by the Auroran Federation Navy. Constructed with the philosophy that “the best defense is a good offense,” the Reisen wields unmatched firepower in the Auroran navy, with few exceptions. Combined with heavy armor relative to her Auroran peers, Reisen excels in prolonged engagements and siege battles.

Unlike most ships in the Sector, Reisen is capable of bringing all her guns to bear on a target thanks to her superfiring turrets. Additionally, she carries advanced rangefinder systems on board, allowing her to sustain fire over much greater distances. The sheer weight of the salvo is enough to rattle any experienced captain, and one must be prepared for an all-or-nothing assault if they wish to approach from the front. However, the same armor that lets her contend with other battleships in the Sector also renders her vulnerable to flanking by smaller, more maneuverable ships, especially wolf-packs with hit-and-run tactics. It is not uncommon to see Reisens accompanied by large numbers of escorts to accommodate for this weakness.

“Stand aside, I’m coming through!” -Captain Will Li, engaging a pirate battle group near Los Scrapyard

Armok Industries' Reflecta technology proved to be invaluable as it's capable of protecting the ship against anything but the most dangerous and volatile weaponry.
The Navy quickly invested untold amount of funding into developing a more economical version of the armor. This led to the creation of Reflecta II which is installable in modular armor plates, now commonly seen in dazzled warships.

“Stand aside, I’m coming through!” -Captain Will Li, engaging a pirate battle group near Los Scrapyard

This ship was given up-to-date military-grade upgrades and a unique camouflage pattern belonging to the Auroran Royal Navy's November Fleet, personally led by the Aeria Charlotte herself. Additional armor plating and durable protective coating were installed to enhance the survivability of this ship; albeit at the cost of slightly lowered maneuverability and speed due to additional weight.

Since the Auroran Federation’s arrival in the Sector, the Rillaru has become the standard by which battlecruisers are judged in the southern Persean Sector, whether its natives like it or not. Famously known for versatility in terms of firepower and agility, along with sleek design aesthetics, Rillaru is the most reliable capital ship to have ever served the Auroran Federation Navy, as well as being a favorite amongst crews and their officers alike. 

While not as durable or as powerful as the Reisen, half of the fleet admirals and captains of the Auroran Navy favor the Rillaru as their flagship, regardless of the fleet composition or formation. What it sacrifices in durability, it makes up for in maneuverability, being incredibly nimble for a ship of her size, able to navigate through large-scale engagements for favorable positions of attack with ease. Captains should not underestimate her firepower either, as she maintains enough firepower to engage any ship from virtually any angle. In the hands of a skilled captain it’s possible to engage in duels with proper battleships and prevail.

A rare variant of the Rillaru, this post-production battlecruiser was upgraded with extended side-mounted weapon platforms next to its flight decks. It is also installed with a reverse-engineered phase skimmer system which would give her unparalleled agility unlike its stock version.

This post-production royal battlecruiser was made on the orders of the Permaisuri herself. The result was a battlecruiser that blended doctrines of both the heavily armed and armored November fleet, and the sleek maneuverability of the Automata Expeditionary Fleet. In particular, she boasts an additional rear turret and some Reflecta II plating, giving her protection and firepower similar to the Reisen IIs, while internally, she has received a number of engine and sensor upgrades, allowing her to maneuver similarly to the Rillaru IIs in spite of the added weight. All these upgrades come at an astronomical cost, and as such, only one vessel has ever been produced, under the flag of her namesake, Permaisuri Aeria Charlotte Yuki.

This ship has been modified differently compared to existing standards. She has been given the classification of 'Royal' type and the first ship to receive this designation.

While the Automata Expeditionary Division excel in hit and run tactics, they are at a severe disadvantage in a head on fight. In the event of a pitched battle, there are few vessels in their inventory that can withstand the might of a proper battleship. In need of an emergency response ship, the Ceyan Grand Armada authorized the development of the Nonayen class hybrid battlecruiser.


In order to satisfy the criteria of a long-range response ship, the Nonayen carries a massive engine block and minimal armor, and as a result, boasts one of the highest top speeds for a capital ship. Once on the scene, the improved Melody Drives divert the no longer needed engine power to shields, helping offset its light armor. Once firmly entrenched, the Nonayen makes swift work of enemy shields and armor with its powerful array of spinal weaponry. While she can tear apart enemy capital ships, her forward facing weapons make her rather vulnerable to flanking attacks from smaller vessels, and an EMP strike to her spinal array can cripple much of her firepower.

A radical addition to the capital formation of the Auroran Federation Navy, this missile battlecruiser entered the prototyping stage with a baptism of fire at the Battle for Aurora. During which she carved a swath of destruction through the opposition, literally and figuratively setting the skies ablaze in a violet inferno. While the production model has since scaled back its destructive capabilities, the ships themselves are often heralded as omens of destruction, and the original prototype that tore open the enemy that fateful day is rumored to still be in service.

The results of that battle were a surprise to many naval strategists who had been originally opposed to the Purcellyra’s creation, as the Auroran Federation had, and still does, pride itself on its ballistic gunnery. The current production model boasts four long-range cruise missile launchers, as well as two additional large hardpoints that, while they can mount ballistic weaponry at the behest of the gun admirals, are commonly sporting the weapon that set the Sector alight all those cycles ago, the Semibreve anti-fleet torpedo. With an internal missile manufactory, automated missile loading systems, and the best point defense capabilities the Federation has to offer, the Purcellyra commands a powerful presence as both the Auroran Federation’s premier missile ship and its best anti-missile defense platform.

“We came in peace, offering our song to the Sector, and they responded with violence. And so for our encore we brought forth Purcellyra, to conduct our symphony of destruction.” - Permaisuri Aeria Charlotte Yuki

The Persephone Natasya is a heavily modified Purcellyra, named after a venerable captain who served as the first generation Taufan wing commander, and recently given an astounding leap in promotion to Vice Admiral. The hull has seen significant changes in various ways to address the Purcellyra’s weaknesses. Namely close quarters combat, as most Purcellyras had little they could do against a battleship closing the distance without frying itself with volatile energies. As a result, its top-side long range cruise missile launchers were instead replaced by large turret emplacements, allowing the Persephone to engage opponents at all ranges safely. To further solidify her role in the main battle line, she also received the newly developed Reflecta plating, granting her vastly improved protection against flanking opponents. Despite all the changes to make her suitable for brawling with battleships, she still maintains a deadly long reach in the shape of the Meitou Semibreve volatile cruise missiles. She now flies in the same iconic color pattern as the ceremonial Aeria Charlotte class Battlecruiser, taking part in the defense of Aurora as Solvernia's dependable enforcer in the November Fleet, though her captain yearns for more than simple patrol duty.

This ship has been modified differently compared to existing standards. She has been given the classification of 'Royal' type and the second ship to receive this designation.

After witnessing the pure might of the alien battleships during the Battle of Aurora cycles ago, it became painfully clear that the Auroran Navy lacked a proper capital ship of its own. Without an armored warship to go toe-to-toe with their enemies’ battleships, their time in the Sector would be short-lived. The Fusoreina was designed as the stopgap solution to this capital problem.

Armok Industries and Manufacturing took the helm on this project, being one of only a few manufacturers capable of ship building to follow the Permaisuri’s expedition into the Sector. Named after one of the CEO’s friends who perished at the Battle of Aurora, the Fusoreina was the first true battleship fielded by the Auroran Navy within the Sector. In addition to heavy armor and abundant firepower, she was the test bed for both the recently reversed engineered damper fields, and AIM’s brand new Reflecta armor project. The result was that Fusoreina boasted unmatched armor protection within the sector.

Despite excelling in her role as a frontline warship, the cost of her armor and development meant she was not adopted into the Auroran Navy. However, her Reflecta armor lives on in the new Reflecta II modular armor plate, now mounted on most November fleet ships, including her replacement the Reisen II. The success of the Fusoreina project did win her many fans, and AIM took measures to reduce her costs while maintaining her performance, creating the Fusoreina II, which has now entered the prototype stage.

 This rare ship is part of the 'Prototypes' that are accepted into limited service of the Auroran Federation Navy. The ship's complex armor plating was deemed to be too expensive to maintain as it requires dedicated logistic support to keep it running which made it heavily reliant on a secured logistic chain from a homeport.  It is painted with the navy dazzle camouflage patterns with gold stripes along the forward bow to signify its prototype status.

Auroran battle doctrine has long been ruled by two schools of thought, strike craft superiority and ballistic gunnery. While the two sides have been at odds since the aircraft carrier's introduction to Auroran waters centuries ago, recently there has been a movement to merge these two tactics together on a single ship. The result was the prototype aviation battleship, the Isenoria.

Given life as a proof of concept, the Isenoria was built off the existing Reisen II hull. During retrofit, she sacrificed some firepower and armor in order to accommodate the facilities required to service and launch fighters, as well as receiving a large open flight deck above her engines. She would not be adopted into the Auroran Navy standard, however, as they already had an effective battle carrier in the form of the Zepporia-series carriers.

All was not lost for supporters of the Isenoria, though. As it turns out, her large aft flight deck was large enough to support the Auroran Navy’s strategic bomber, the Rafflesya, which, despite dwarfing the Nakiha, did not have the operational range to leave Aurora and engage the Federation’s enemies. The Isenoria continues to be built in limited quantities, now the proud mothership of one of the most powerful bombers within the Auroran arsenal, and one of the most feared ships by planetary and station administrators alike.

This rare ship is part of the 'Prototypes' that are declined into main service of the Auroran Federation Navy, but still chosen to be produced in limited numbers. It is painted with the navy dazzle camouflage patterns with gold stripes along the forward bow to signify its prototype status.

Prior to the introduction of the Reisen, the Auroran Navy was in dire need of a ship that could  participate in extended long-distance engagements. With the Fusoreina still in development, and several cycles before the Reisen would be designed, the Auroran Navy decided that instead it would shorten engagement times by creating a weapon that could devastate ships in one shot.

A scaled up version of the existing Tona-series railgun, the Tona-IV proved to be remarkably effective. Delivering a powerful projectile that could penetrate clean through an enemy ship, and virtually shattering any shield it came across, the Tona-IV would be slated for use in devastating enemy capital ships and orbital defenses. Unfortunately, in order to meet expectations, the weapon became far too large to mount on existing ships, defeating the purpose of the project. Unwilling to let these capital killers go to waste, the Auroran Navy hastily designed the Sentry artillery cruiser. Designed around the Tona-IV, the original Sentry was little more than a set of engines strapped to the cannon itself. Nonetheless, properly escorted, she proved extraordinarily efficient at dealing with capital ships in live fire exercises. In order to ensure she could operate semi-autonomously, the Sentry received various upgrades, including point defenses and an MLRS system for engaging small to mid-size targets at closer ranges.

Learning from the Tona-IV, the Auroran Navy used the lessons learned in the development of the Sentry to create the Phenex railcannon turret. While smaller, she was still effective enough to pass clean through ships, and would be mounted on the future dreadnought, the Solvernia. The Sentry performed admirably at the Battle of Aurora, and while she shined in her given role, it reminded naval planners of her glaring weakness: her limited firing arcs. Having lost her escorts in the chaos, the Sentry was prone to being outmaneuvered and destroyed. After the battle, the Fusoreina would finish development, and the Reisen would enter the prototyping stage. The Sentry would resume production, but only in limited quantities. They now stand vigil over the Federation’s bases.

“You may fire when ready.” Captain Will “Huff” Tolkin

This variant of the Sentry primarily serves as a fleet support weapon, primarily as an anti-strike platform. Armed with 4 Sakura VLS missile batteries, this Sentry can flood the battlespace with numerous missiles, making approaching it with small ships and strikecraft an incredibly risky endeavour. This variant would first see deployment at Federation bases shortly after the Battle of Aurora. This rare ship is part of the 'Prototypes' that are declined into service of the Auroran Federation Navy, but still chosen to be produced in limited numbers.
