This variant of the Sentry serves as a mobile anti-ship battery. Unlike the standard variant that stands vigils over the Federation’s various footholds in space, this one sacrifices two of its missile launchers for the facilities needed to mount and operate a second railcannon. This makes the Sentry exceptionally dangerous to enemy capital ships, where its cannons can be fired in an alternating pattern for constant shield pressure, or together for an immense alpha strike to destroy enemy shields. This rare ship is part of the 'Prototypes' that are declined into service of the Auroran Federation Navy, but still chosen to be produced in limited numbers.

The Eyeseau class is an auxiliary command ship that serves as a mobile hub for overall fleet communications, as well as serving as the Auroran Navy’s primary electronic warfare platform and early-warning radar. Based off of Yurian AWACS platforms that dominated the airspace of the Unifying War.

Commonly found in Auroran system defense fleets, these ships use their powerful radar arrays in order to detect man-made objects, usually pirates or would-be invaders, although they have also been seen used for search-and-rescue, locating stranded ships with accuracy much greater than standard Neutrino sensors. These same radars also contribute to her self-defense as well, allowing her to emit powerful jamming signals, disrupting enemy tracking and communications.

These powerful arrays come at a cost, the expenses limiting her production run, as well as requiring constant maintenance to keep them at optimal performance. Nonetheless, she has firmly entrenched herself as a vital part of any commander’s fleet, becoming the embodiment of the phrase “You can run, but you can’t hide.”

“Our hunt ends this day, pirate! There is nowhere you can flee that we cannot follow!” -Commander Genos Zalvus, pursuing an Entiaris raiding party

This rare ship is one of the prototypes not seen in offical Auroran Federation Navy service, but still chosen to be produced in limited numbers. It is painted with the navy's "dazzle" camouflage patterns with gold stripes along the forward bow to signify its prototype status.

The Sunami landing cruiser is an oddity amongst the Sector. Her primary purpose is to safely deliver Auroran Marines to the combat zone, and to provide close range fire support. Unlike the Valkyrie troop transports, however, the Sunami also engages orbital defenses alongside the rest of the fleet as a long range support ship.

The Sunami is under equipped for ship to ship combat with opponents of equal size, as much of her compartments are dedicated to housing her vast complement of marines and vehicles. Additionally, her flight deck allows her to support atmospheric flight operations, enabling the use of normally in-atmosphere only aircraft. Combined, the Sunami carries enough landing forces to sweep aside any opposition on the ground, much like the natural disaster for which she is named for, turning the tide of any battle.

Despite excelling in her role, the Auroran Navy utilizes the Machi-series of troop transports, which were both cheaper and faster to produce. The Sunami still sees use by special forces, where her signature Rogue Wave battle strategy allows her to seize critical objectives faster than any other ship.

“One in the rubble, another in the rafters! The rest of you, follow me!” -Sergeant Gordo leading a ground assault into an enemy fortress.

 This rare ship is part of the 'Prototypes' that are accepted into limited service of the Auroran Federation Navy. A superior alternative was proposed and come in the form of Machi APA series which is cheaper and simpler to mass produce. It is painted with the navy dazzle camouflage patterns with gold stripes along the forward bow to signify its prototype status.

Developed after the United Aurora Federation’s arrival in the sector, this prototype destroyer is a highly specialized fleet disruption vessel. While she lacks direct firepower, its presence can be felt in any large fleet engagement. This is due to its signature weapon system’s unique properties, namely a unique implosion/explosion tandem warhead, earning it the nickname “The Lion’s Roar”. It was developed in response to the exotic technologies witnessed within the Persean Sector. What resists destruction from tactical breve strikes and copious saturation of projectiles, should be made reluctant to do so.

The Lyora is designed to break formations in large fleet engagements with its main armament. The large projectile is reinforced with heavy armor to carry its volatile payload into an enemy formation. The contents of this payload are rumored to be a weaponized by-product of classified Auroran research in the pastry industry. Detonation of this payload forms an extremely brief and unstable singularity, knocking out ship systems and pulling surrounding objects in. Following the implosion, a concussive blast scatters whatever mass has been collected. While absolutely devastating to a tight formation given the likely chance of violent collision, the Lion's Roar is somewhat ineffective at dealing with singular vessels as it lacks significant explosive power like its more potent anti-material cousin, Semibreve. The reinforced armor necessitates an enlarged engine that lacks the fine gimbal system of a typical anti-ship missile. Therefore, the Lyora captain should take into account the inertia of their vessel before firing the Lion's Roar. Field tests have determined that proficient usage of the weapon is difficult for an untrained captain, and sufficient caution should be used in deployment.

She followed a remarkably tempestuous development cycle. After the massive Auroran naval armor refurbishment and reclassification, several vessels had their classes updated to better reflect their roles within the fleet. Afterwards, the Auroran Navy found themselves without a single ship classified as a destroyer. Low ranking officials within the navy bantered that they'd never have a proper destroyer before they were all back on Aurora. Undeterred by jests, development of the Lyora demonstrated the resilience of the Auroran engineering teams assigned to the project. When design parameters became erratic, the fabled Armok Industries and Manufacturing (AIM) was called in to help finalize the blueprint. Despite the turbulent development, and limited production due to prototype design costs, she has become the first new destroyer class vessel within the newly re-organized Auroran Federation Navy.

This rare ship is part of the 'Prototypes' that are declined into main service of the Auroran Federation Navy, but still chosen to be produced in limited numbers. It is painted with the navy dazzle camouflage patterns with gold stripes along the forward bow to signify its prototype status.

Soon after their arrival in the Persean Sector and the integration of the Automata Expeditionary Division, the United Auroran Federation found itself harassed constantly by an unknown force. Survivors’ reports stated that these assailants were hard to spot on sensors, and moved much faster than any known fleet. Some reports even state battleships materialized out of the void at such close ranges, that there was no time to respond to the sudden assault. When Auroran High Command began organizing a task force, the AED, which had been tracking the unknown assailants for some time, stepped forward and unveiled a ship they had been working on in secret: the Black Sorceress, made specifically to track and combat the anomalies.

Using the Aeria Charlotte as a base, the Black Sorceress was larger and more heavily armed than her predecessor, although the extra weight has slowed her down considerably. These traits have made her closer to a battleship, albeit a lightly armored one. In order to mitigate this weakness, special systems were made in order to improve her survivability. The first was a one of a kind Shinonian Scatter Array, which spreads unstable energy particles around the ship, disrupting missile guidance systems. The second was a unique time dilation system based on phase technology recovered from Persean native ships. While it does not send the ship into another dimension, it still enables the ship to operate on an accelerated time scale, helping her mitigate her reduced speed, as well as allowing her to improve her volume of fire for a short period.

Interestingly, there were originally supposed to be two vessels of this class. However, the processing requirements for operating the time dilation system and the scatter array proved too great for a single Crown Princess model to handle alone. As a result, the second ship was scrapped in order to move her core to the existing ship, resulting in a unique ship, even among the standard AED fleet, possessing a twin-core. While they work in harmony, there have been some incidents caused by friction between the two cores, possibly arising from bitterness over the second’s scrapped ship.

Every navy has a name passed down through generations of ships. For Aurorans, they revere one name in particular, which has been carried by 6 ships prior. And soon, their enemies will come to fear it. The Solvernia, nameship and only ship of the Solvernia-VII class dreadnoughts, 7th to bear the name, and pride of the Auroran Navy.

They would get its first taste of the dreadnought, not within their own territory, but in orbit over the Federation’s Homeworld, Aurora. She still had yet to be commissioned when an alien fleet arrived through the gate. To call her first moments out of the dockyards a trial by fire was an understatement. Still unproven and untested, Solvernia responded to the aggression by penetrating the first battleship she could get her hands on with her massive 88cm rail-cannons. Having pumped her first victim full of nuclear projectiles, the Solvernia would lead a counterattack, thrusting deep into the enemy fleet. Her presence demanded the enemy’s undivided attention, where her nigh impervious armor absorbed vigorous blows that would have ruined any other ship, and her powerful Melody fusion reactors granted her shields the stamina needed to weather such a vigorous pounding. 

While the planet was ravaged by the battle, Solvernia’s domination of the battlespace prevented the worst of it. Outrage at these unknown adversaries swept through the myriad peoples of Aurora like a wildfire. While her mission had originally been one of peace and exploration, the former research expedition became one searching for answers… and for some, vengeance. With Solvernia at the tip of the spear, the expedition would set forth beyond the gate… Into the Persean Sector.

“The Solvernia is the embodiment of our combined might and prosperity. She extends open arms to those who wish for a new age of peace, and will simultaneously be the end of any ship, station, or civilization who dares impede our autonomy. Respect existence or expect resistance; this I forewarn to all sovereign leaders across space, time, and beyond. The Aurorans see well past the event horizon, and nothing in this universe, or any universe, will stand in our way.” Aeria Charlotte Yuki, Permaisuri of the United Auroran Federation, Solvernia’s commissioning ceremony, 48 hours after the Battle of Aurora.

The Aurorans had intended for Solvernia for peace, to protect the Federation’s expeditions as they charted their courses throughout the Persean Sector. The Battle of Aurora changed that, transforming Solvernia from a testament of unity into a symbol of righteous indignation, an arbiter of justice. And so, now unwillingly settled into their new home within the Sector, the United Auroran Federation began work on a new symbol of their ideals: the Novaeria.

With the Solvernia becoming the flagship of the elite November Fleet, now so closely associated with the military might of the Federation, the Novaeria was designed to be her anti-thesis as the flagship of the Automata Expeditionary Division. Whereas Solvernia was made to protect the expeditions, Novaeria conducted them herself, given sensor suites and communications arrays as opposed to Solvernia’s armor and firepower. To assist her in these endeavors, she was given vast hangars, sprawling flight decks, and enormous docking clamps to service the many vessels that conducted surveys on her behalf, large and small. To many she would become a home away from home, a true “mother” ship for all exploration expeditions.

Despite being an envoy of peace, she was by no means defenseless. The same docking bays that serviced the survey ships could easily accommodate the massive Reisen battleships, or even her warrior counterpart, the Solvernia herself. Her many hangars and flight decks could unleash a veritable horde of strike craft, some large enough to even launch frigates and destroyers. If Solvernia brought peace to the Federation, Novaeria would maintain it, boldly going where none dared travel before. Whether she brings with her explorers or soldiers, would only be determined with time.

“With my creation, so ends the era of Persean meddling in our affairs. I mark the beginning of infinity. Our endless era of peace.” - [REDACTED]

Deep space exploration is not an endeavor to be taken lightly. Prior to the Battle of Aurora, there was no confirmation of any life beyond their little planet. Those going boldly into the unknown would need to supply themselves for extended periods of time, likely with no contact. Thus a vessel was made, the first testament to the might of the Federation’s industry, the Cherry Vanguard hyperfreighter.

The project was met with considerable resistance when it was unveiled. To construct a ship of this size was a massive undertaking, one that many nations of Aurora viewed as far too costly. In addition to this opposition, she faced some problems during construction as well. Her planned shields were far less efficient than originally calculated, resulting in both a much higher power demand and maintenance cost. The Permaisuri, however, had a solution, unveiling the first Macro Melody Drive. The Cherry Vanguard would also have some of her cargo space devoted to new flux batteries, designed for increased flux capacity and long term storage, to accommodate the massive increase in power generation. While this would lower her operational endurance by an estimated 20%, the new reactor allowed her to increase her operational range even further by vastly improving her thrust. With the Permaisuri’s willingness to contribute her own nation’s top secret research, the rest of the world saw just how far she was willing to take this project to completion. Opposing leaders would reluctantly stand down in the face of popular support for the project, and the Cherry Vanguard would see completion on schedule.

Cherry Vanguard would be absent during the Battle of Aurora, reaching the end of her first endurance trial shortly after the battle’s conclusion. Upon learning of the events, her crew would be amongst the first to volunteer to travel beyond the gate. And true to her name, she would be the expedition’s vanguard into the depths of the Persean Sector, becoming the fleet’s home away from home in enemy territory, until the colonization of New Aurora.

During the early days after being stranded in the Sector, some of the combat reports provided by Automata recon units began mentioning ships of unknown origin. These ships were almost alien by design, and far more powerful compared to anything the Sector could muster. This forced the Aurorans to acknowledge that there was a far greater threat to their existence than their present opposition, one that even put the technological advantage of Tri-Tachyon to shame. With the standard Auroran doctrine of Fighter Superiority proving to be ineffective against this new threat, they turned to their older, still heavily relied upon philosophy: use more guns.

Originally designed as an experimental battleship project for the Automata Expeditionary Division, this super capital warship met the November doctrine of "Overwhelming Superior Firepower" while providing improved flexibility of Automata designs. A finely wrought fusion of two long thought-to-be incompatible doctrines, she was not without flaws.

Boasting tremendous firepower in the form of her Meitou Edge Type 4 main battery guns, she could deal significant suppressive damage at far greater range, especially when overcharged. However, the sheer size of the weapon required a sacrifice of the ship's small caliber weaponry, including a majority of its point defense systems. This is somewhat mitigated by its hangar bay allowing the deployment of interceptors and extending its engagement range by employing a siege module at the expense of mobility. Ultimately, however, the ship was far more costly than its in-service counterpart, the Rillaru-II, which delivered much of the same firepower for a fraction of the cost, while being considerably more maneuverable to boot.

Nevertheless, any upcoming adventurous investor may stumble upon this forgotten prototype and would find the ship worth every penny so long as it isn't thrown into impossible battles. She may not hold a candle to the mighty 'Warrior Princess' but if supported by enough friendlies - she will one day, be triumphant and worthy of that recognition denied after so long.

This ship has been retrofitted and heavily modified to meet the specifications required by the Auroran Automata Expeditionary Division. It's equipped with the latest avionics and prototype upgrades, allowing it to outperform its predecessors while also boasting a lower sensor profile, thanks to the stealth technology behind its durable and unique AED coating.

The Exoria-class Spacecraft Carrier is one of the oldest ship classes still in service in the Auroran arsenal. This class has a long and fabled history, with designs hailing back to the days of seabound aircraft carriers, including the once-iconic exposed flight decks synonymous with carriers of that age. While seemingly counterintuitive in the era of Space Exploration, it allows for faster and safer recovery of fighter craft and pilots that any other carrier would consider already lost.

The older design philosophies and atmospheric capabilities have also simplified production considerably, allowing planetside facilities to produce these ships en-masse, a feat shared with the venerable Nakiha-class frigates. This factor would in fact prove pivotal during the Battle of Aurora, where newly-built Exorias would bear hastily repurposed atmospheric fighter craft into combat, allowing the Aurorans to maintain air superiority in spite of their age. These same aircraft would later become precursors to the now well-renowned Auroran fighter wings, such as the Surreal and the Exoscar. Likewise, the success of both the Nakiha and the Exoria led to the creation of the Minoria, the Federation’s first fast response carrier.

To this day, the Exoria remains in use, in defense of Auroran fleets within Auroran space and beyond.

This ship has been retrofitted and heavily modified to meet the specifications required by the Auroran Automata Expeditionary Division. It's equipped with the latest avionics and prototype upgrades, allowing it to outperform its predecessors while also a boasting lower sensor profile, thanks to the stealth technology behind its durable and unique AED coating.

After the Battle of Aurora, it became clear that the Exorias, while ideal for patrols and pursuing pirate raiders, proved to be too slow to keep up with their escorts.When the Battle for Aurora broke out, its opening moments were nearly disastrous for the Auroran Navy, as the Nakihas arrived with little air cover, only holding on thanks to their superior maneuverability and speed. With the need for a carrier to be able to keep up with these frigates, the United Auroran Federation designed the Minoria.

Based on the Nakiha hull, using lessons learned from the Exoria fast-carriers, the Minoria has been a powerful addition to the Auroran fleet. In order to carry the systems and hangars required to service aircraft, virtually all armaments on the original hull were removed, save for a few point defense systems. However, what she lacks in firepower, she gains in flexibility, being able to field virtually any of Aurora’s feared strike craft, ranging from the blistering fast Revere interceptors, to the powerful Exoscar torpedo bombers, or even the favorite among carrier captains, the Super Aria multi-role fighters.

Like both the Exoria and the Nakiha, the Minoria retains her predecessors’ ability to launch from within the planet’s atmosphere. With her small size, planet side landing zones for these escort carriers were now feasible. When New Aurora was established within the Persean Sector, regions were allotted for the stationing Minorias and Nakihas, allowing a swift reaction force for any potential aggressors.

This ship was given up-to-date military-grade upgrades and a unique camouflage pattern belonging to the Auroran Royal Navy's November Fleet, personally led by the Aeria Charlotte herself. Additional armor plating and durable protective coating were installed to enhance the survivability of this ship; albeit at the cost of slightly lowered maneuverability and speed due to additional weight.

This ship has been retrofitted and heavily modified to meet the specifications required by the Auroran Automata Expeditionary Division. It's equipped with the latest avionics and prototype upgrades, allowing it to outperform its predecessors while also a boasting lower sensor profile, thanks to the stealth technology behind its durable and unique AED coating.

Automata ships are designed to maintain a low sensor profile and to survey the unknown reaches of hyperspace. While Novaeria would be appointed as the Division flagship, her sheer size prevented her from hiding effectively. Seeking to provide the same capabilities scaled down to be more practical and economical, the Auroran Expeditionary Automata Division began development on their first dedicated ship following this doctrine, the Neuxoria.

Many of the design features included on the Neuxoria were lessons learned from other carriers in the Federations arsenal. Years of development allowed many of the aircraft servicing facilities to be automated or scaled down, allowing for increased hangar space. In addition, the Automata conversion of existing Auroran ships helped refine Neuko programming, allowing for reduced crew sizes and better aircraft maintenance routines. These upgrades reduced her power consumption, indirectly contributing to her smaller sensor profile on top of her standard Automata stealth technologies.

Arguably her most important upgrade was the sensor suite. Originally developed for Novaeria, the Neuxoria received a powerful data-uplink array, allowing to network with all her fighters, allowing her to coordinate attacks with frightening precision. Swift for a capital ship of her size, she can effectively disengage much faster than her November counterpart. Combined with her stealth capabilities, the Neuxoria has become a frightening presence on the battlefield… or rather lack of presence. As a result, while she is designed to support exploration expeditions, it is not uncommon for the Neuxoria to be pressed into service for black operations, due to her ability to strike swiftly with her aircraft and seemingly fade away.

This ship has been retrofitted and heavily modified to meet the specifications required by the Auroran Automata Expeditionary Division. It's equipped with the latest avionics and prototype upgrades, allowing it to outperform its predecessors while also a boasting lower sensor profile, thanks to the stealth technology behind its durable and unique AED coating.

The Battle of Aurora provided many lessons for the Federation Navy. While it was won on the backs of the Nakiha frigates and Exoria carriers, the Exoria proved to be woefully ill-equipped for withstanding the firepower of capital ship, meaning Auroran defense fleets had a very real possibility of having their air power crippled if the enemy focused the hapless carrier. With a need for a carrier that could support the Solvernia in the main battle line, the Royal November Fleet set out to design the Zepporia-II fleet battle carrier.

The Zepporia was built with the focus of holding her ground. She is even capable of maintaining a virtually constant air presence, as her streamlined launch and recovery systems allow for swift re-arming times.Her pilots are some of the best the Federation has to offer, trained in evasive maneuvers ensuring a high survival rate. It is not uncommon for Zepporias to be home to multiple fighter ace squadrons. But the true testament to her might is actually her survivability. Boasting armor similar to a Reisen, and protected by Reflecta-II armor plates, the Zepporia is capable of going toe-to-toe even with proper battleships.

This focus on defense has had a crippling effect on her mobility: She is slower than even the Reisen battleships. However, once she’s entrenched, she is virtually unmovable, earning her place as the Shield of Aurora, in orbit over the UAF's new home. 

This ship was given up-to-date military-grade upgrades and a unique camouflage pattern belonging to the Auroran Royal Navy's November Fleet which is led personally by the Queen of Yuria herself. Additional armor plating and durable protective coating were installed to enhance the survivability of this ship; albeit at the cost of slightly lowered maneuverability and speed due to additional weight.

Prior to the introduction of the Zepporia class fleet carriers, the Milkydromeda class were the primary fleet carriers of the United Aurora Federation. Built with the lessons of Yurian naval domination during the Unifying War in mind, the Milkydromeda class fielded one of the largest strikecraft compliments ever known. Inside the ship, absurd amounts of personnel and equipment have been dedicated solely to arming and servicing the hundreds of strike craft on board, allowing for even the most heavily armed strikecraft to be armed and prepped for launch on a moments’ notice.

The massive flight deck is not just for show. Sporting numerous launch and retrieval systems, she can launch and recover an astounding number of squadrons simultaneously. Beneath that, two decks worth of hangars, one doubling as a secondary flight deck, allowing for a second set of squadrons to be launched, effectively doubling the amount in the air at any given time. The ship also maintains two flight controllers, one located on the island on the main flight deck, and another on the lower flight deck. This allows for multiple orders to be given to the squadrons simultaneously. The spacious hangars and specialized workshops also allow for proper care and maintenance of the Federation’s numerous models of gunships far from home, allowing the Milkydromeda class to carry some of the heaviest airborne firepower available with only one downside. Despite her stellar performance as a carrier, her focus on carrying and maintaining all varieties of strikecraft leaves her with woefully inadequate point-defense systems. This left the ship vulnerable to harassment, requiring large numbers of escorts to keep her safe. While this may have been suitable for the aquatic navy of the Unifying War, in the age of three-dimensional combat, this weakness was magnified. As such, the Milkydromeda-class fleet carrier was not suitable as an escort to Solvernia. The Milkydromeda-class carriers were left behind at Aurora during the expedition to the Persean Sector. Mostly.

The Mooparia is the sole Milkydromeda class carrier in the Sector. Present at the Battle of Aurora as the flagship of the Misthaven fleet, she has seen her fair share of combat. While in transit to join the Solvernia on the expedition to the Persean Sector, the flag of Misthaven was transferred to one of the new Zepporia class carriers. With no time to arrange for berthing at Aurora, the Mooparia was instead given the task of acting as a strikecraft tender, carrying vast amounts of spare Auroran strikecraft for the Zepporias within the expedition fleet. With the Federation stranded for the time being, she has since been relegated as a training facility for new pilots, though her systems remain ready for war, should the need arise.

The widespread piracy problem along the Freedom’s Passage saw the United Aurora Federation cooperated with the Southwestern Worlds in conducting anti-piracy operations, particularly with the San Batavia Republic. When a Zepporia-class supercarrier undergoing anti-piracy operations suffered a devastating ambush from a barrage of Reaper torpedoes and rendered inoperable, the wreck was towed to the nearest system, which was the Europa Star System. Assessment at Vareendal Military Shipyard originally deemed her as “irreparable”, citing the extensive damage where “practically half of her hull was gone” and thus slated for scrapping. However, interests in repurposing the “still somewhat intact” hull convinced the Stadhouder of SBR, Clarisse van den Graaf, to instead favour her rebuild into an assault supercarrier capable of supplementing the existing Morningstar-class supercarriers. Further discussions between the Aurorans and the San Batavians thus resulted in the Constelyria assault supercarrier.

The overhaul, repair and refit works at Vareendal saw the Zepporia’s design radically changed into an unique blend of Auroran and San Batavian design philosophies. The lost portions of the hull were reworked and replaced with San Batavian parts and systems, notably those related to her carrier operations. Exposed and angled flight decks and the advanced Recall Device ship systems were her key reworks, allowing rapid deployment of strikecrafts. Extensive overhaul and improvements to the hangar system allowed for both the implementation of Auroran and San Batavian strikecraft doctrines, further increasing efficiency and reducing crew requirement. However, the modified hull structure and lacks of Reflecta-II armour plates meant the Constelyria’s protection was inferior compared to the original Zepporia. At some point, the experimental Garuda 808 XLC positron cannon was fitted onboard as a frontal spinal weapon, solidifying her role as an assault supercarrier. 

The Constelyria re-entered service as a San Batavia Republican Navy’s battlecarrier and participated in anti-piracy operations until her eventual repatriation back to the Aurorans. The spinal cannon proved to be remarkably popular amongst the San Batavians as well as the Aurorans who witnessed the cannon in action. Proposals for future battlecarriers to adopt a similar approach or upgrading existing Zepporia to the Constelyria still persist to this day.

The Nebula-Bravo is the oldest ship in the Federation, predating the Exoria and even the Federation itself. Inspired by an old Annabellen smuggler trick, where a majority of their “cargo ships” during their War of Independence were in fact barges towed by powerful tugs. What people call the Nebula-Bravo is actually two separate entities: an expendable cargo hold, and the ship itself. The idea being that if the ship was in danger of being destroyed, and its cargo expendable, the ship could detach itself from the cargo hold and escape on its own. When adopted into service by the then newly-formed United Aurora Federation, it was expected to be replaced by the then in development Junyo-Delta super freighter. The Junyo-Delta would be adopted by civilian industries, however the Nebula-Bravo remained in service with the military for ease of logistics. 

While she does not have as high a cargo capacity as her civilian counterpart, she could easily outrun most vessels. Additionally, the modular nature of the cargo hold allowed for a single Nebula to drop off one cargo unit and pick up another in a matter of minutes, vastly improving loading and unloading times by allowing them to occur before the freighter arrives and after it departs respectively. Due to the vital nature of these supplies, ejection of cargo to give pursuers the slip is quite rare in the age of space exploration. To compensate, the “expendable” cargo holds were outfitted with their own booster modules, assisting the Nebula-Bravo in its escape with a temporary boost of speed.

This rare ship is part of the 'Prototypes' that are declined into main service of the Auroran Federation Navy, but still chosen to be produced in limited numbers. It is painted with the navy dazzle camouflage patterns with gold stripes along the forward bow to signify its prototype status.

The Neoyselif is the black sheep of Vanguard Enterprises, being its first, and only, design to be given to other shipbuilders. Originally conceived as the super-freighter to serve Aurora while the Cherry Vanguard took the bulk of freighters for her construction, the development of the Neoyselif was so absurd that if you told anyone, most would call you insane. The ship ended up only looking like a Vanguard design on the exterior thanks to many alterations made by the shipbuilders. Due to the massive scandals that arose from this, Vanguard never collaborated with other shipbuilders after the completion of the only ship in its class.

To begin with, the ship is well over 5 times the originally planned dimensions, dwarfing the already massive cargo modules of the Nebula series. While this meant she carried more cargo, it meant building her en masse was already impractical from the start. While the massive engines gave her an impressive top speed for a ship her size, her sheer girth still made her an easy target. Instead of reducing her to her planned dimensions, the shipbuilders opted to break from Vanguard’s design code of simply outrunning the enemy to allow her to fight back. This of course meant armor and guns, and the amount of armor needed to properly protect her saw build costs skyrocket even further. Magazines and turrets were mounted all over, and in the end, she appeared to be more of a battleship than a cargo freighter.

The completion of Neoyselif did not stop her from draining Vanguard’s pockets further. Upon launch, the shipbuilders assigned a captain fresh out of navigational school and hired a rather dubious crew. Eager to show off, the fresh-faced captain jumped the gun on launch. The ship, still connected to the dock, tore out docking clamps and fuel stations from the sudden acceleration. Smaller vessels, who thought the launch was much later, were caught by surprise as the Neoyselif emerged from what should have been a clear lane of traffic, smashing into the side of the armored freighter’s hull. Port authorities proceeded to seize the vessel, and the fines and lawsuits ensured that Vanguard would never entrust its projects to others again.

Auroran tanker design had been stagnant for a long time. The recent developments in hyperspace travel meant that the Federation had to design new tankers capable of surviving the rigors of hyperspace storms and storing anti-matter… and with the Battle of Aurora fresh in everyone’s minds, able to survive the trials of combat. KOKIA Industries would helm this project, the first in the series being the Sekai-class armored tanker.

While supposedly a logistics ship, the Sekai is closer to a frigate with an oversized fuel tank than a tanker. With armor to rival actual military ships of its size, and weaponry  heavy enough to scare off similarly sized vessels, the Sekai has, unsurprisingly, claimed a few combat victories in self-defense. Helping it is the fact that its uncharacteristically aggressive design often sees it mistaken for an actual combat vessel. Pirates target the hapless “non-military” targets, leaving the Sekai free to escape unnoticed… or, more often than not, engage enemies alongside its escorts. The “lack of tankers” has reportedly also scared away some small-time pirates, as it means there must surely be a much larger fleet nearby.

Auroran tanker design had been stagnant for a long time. The recent developments in hyperspace travel meant that the Federation had to design new tankers capable of surviving the rigors of hyperspace storms and storing anti-matter… and with the Battle of Aurora fresh in everyone’s minds, able to survive the trials of combat. KOKIA Industries would helm this project, the second in the series being the Wo-class armored tanker.

Many of the design decisions found on the Sekai appear here on the Wo as well, including armor comparable to actual military vessels, and unusually heavy armament for a logistic ship. While pound for pound less heavily armed than her little sister, she is still a popular choice for being more economically efficient while maintaining speed. Coupled with her larger than normal fuel tank for a tanker her size, the Wo is perhaps one of the most efficient mid-sized tankers available, while still being more than capable of defending herself.

Auroran tanker design had been stagnant for a long time. The recent developments in hyperspace travel meant that the Federation had to design new tankers capable of surviving the rigors of hyperspace storms and storing antimatter… and with the Battle of Aurora fresh in everyone’s minds, able to survive the trials of combat. KOKIA Industries would helm this project, the last in the series being the Tsutsumu-class armored tanker.

Following the pattern set by the Sekai and the Wo, the Tsutsumu is heavily armed and armored for a logistics ship. Perhaps most surprisingly, KOKIA Industries opted to lean fully into the fact that it looks more like a battleship than a tanker, boasting armor even heavier than the venerable Reisen battleships. With the capability to carry weaponry comparable to a battlecruiser and her own escort fighters, pirates often mistake her as the convoy’s flagship, and give her a wide berth, if they choose to engage at all. This has backfired a couple times, as pirate armadas sometimes misidentify her as the primary threat, but over all, her looming presence has kept convoys safe from attack.

The Battle of Aurora provided many lessons for the Federation Navy. While it was won on the backs of the Nakiha frigates and Exoria carriers, the Exoria proved to be woefully ill-equipped for withstanding the firepower of capital ship, meaning Auroran defense fleets had a very real possibility of having their air power crippled if the enemy focused the hapless carrier. The Solvernia and her escorts can only be in so many places at once, so R&D set out to design a battle carrier  cheap enough to deploy enmasse and powerful enough to go toe to toe with enemy cruisers on her own. The result was the standard battle carrier, the Shinoria.

As a heavy cruiser design modified for aviation purposes, Shinoria is officially classified as an aviation cruiser. Of course, her avionics systems come at a cost. The space required for hangar bays mean the ship itself is undergunned for a Federation ship her size, and her relatively low flux capacity means she’s easily overwhelmed if captains are not mindful of her flux usage. As a result, the need for maneuverability means she had to sacrifice armor, making her rather fragile compared to her contemporaries. Nonetheless, these weaknesses can easily be offset. Built to deliver and support her fighter wings to the front of an engagement, the Shinoria can field a wide variety of strike craft, ranging from the ever-present Super Aria, to the lumbering, but vastly more dangerous Hyacinth saturation bombers, and powerful engines means that she can quickly maneuver into position, or disengage from combat with ease.


This ship was given up-to-date military-grade upgrades, converting it into a missile battlecarrier and a unique camouflage pattern belonging to the Auroran Royal Navy's November Fleet which is led personally by the Queen of Yuria herself. Additional armor plating and durable protective coating were installed to enhance the survivability of this ship; albeit at the cost of slightly lowered maneuverability and speed due to additional weight.

One of the oldest designs in service, the Inazuma pre-dates the Federation, built under international treaty limitations prior to the Auroran formation. Her designers, Ceyae’s famed Inazuma Shipbuilding Company, wanted to ensure that she had the upper hand in firepower, while staying within the limits by the international committee. Competing with Akatsuzuma Shipworks, ISC designed the Inazuma to be under-armored, but over-gunned.

The result was a fragile light cruiser that could overwhelm the opposition thanks to her arrowhead design allowing to concentrate all her firepower forward, at least in theory. Her initial performance was poor, as commanders of the time were used to pre-treaty cruisers being able to take more punishment. As a result she almost didn’t make the cut. Then high command underwent a massive restructuring following the formation of the United Aurora Federation. The new command began using the Inazuma in a fire support role instead of the main battle line, and both her survivability and effectiveness in combat increased tenfold. The Inazuma would be adopted into the fleet, alongside her competitor, the Akatsuzuma, as point defense and fire support for the Federation’s Rillaru fleet. Her forward-focused design would later influence the creation of the Reisen.

With the treaty that limited her annulled during the UAF’s formation, the Inazuma II received significant upgrades in order to improve her survivability. Chief among these was the development of Reflecta armor, which would be mounted to her forward arrowhead. While still fragile by November standards, the Reflecta dramatically improves her survivability, allowing her to be even more of a persistent threat while supporting the fleet's capital ships.

This ship was given up-to-date military-grade upgrades and a unique camouflage pattern belonging to the Auroran Royal Navy's November Fleet which is led personally by the Queen of Yuria herself. Additional armor plating and durable protective coating were installed to enhance the survivability of this ship; albeit at the cost of slightly lowered maneuverability and speed due to additional weight.

One of the oldest designs in service, the Akatsuzuma pre-dates the Federation, built under international treaty limitations prior to the UAF’s formation. Her designers, Ceyae’s then-rising Akatsuzuma Shipworks, designed her with balance in mind, an all around ship that could perform just as well solo as with the fleet. Competing with the Inazuma Shipbuilding Company, ASW designed the Akatsuzuma to be fast in order to compensate for her general lack of armor.

The ship's high-speed and thin armor saw her added to patrol fleets instead of the main battle line, as intended. This would be a blessing in disguise, as she ended up having a stronger initial performance than projected as she excelled in this role as a patrol group flagship. When the United Aurora Federation was formed, and its high command was restructured, the Akatsuzuma would also begin seeing use as an escort, a role which she would share with her rival, the Inazuma.

With the treaty that limited her annulled during the UAF’s formation, the Akatsuzuma II underwent upgrades to expand her role as a guerilla warfare cruiser. Despite this, she hardly gained any weight at all, as more armor was sacrificed in order to help maintain speed while sporting additional weaponry. With Automata stealth technology onboard, the Akatsuzuma became the ship of choice for black ops, striking swiftly before vanishing into the depths of space.

While the Rillaru is the poster child for Auroran shipbuilding abroad in the Persean Sector, the people of the United Auroran Federation are far more familiar with the Lillaru heavy cruiser. Despite being known as “Rillaru’s little sister,” Lillaru is actually the older of the two. She boasts one of the longest production runs for Auroran ships, with initial designs dating all the way back to the Yurian Navy during the Unifying War.

Until the introduction of the Rillaru, the Lillaru would be Aurora’s most heavily armed ship for almost a decade. The original Lillarus served as seagoing battlecruisers, capable of carrying the powerful Cyrexa 452mm railcannons, then newly-designed by Rexa Industries. While these weapons are par for the course of space warfare, back then, they were the largest guns available to any warship, and in an age of shieldless combat, the most deadly.

During the transition to space warfare, these powerful battlecruisers were retired. At this time the United Aurora Federation was still in its infancy, and Yuria, having won the global conflict, needed a ship to maintain the peace while their new battlecruiser project finished production. And so, the Lillaru was reintroduced into the Auroran Navy. Reclassified as a heavy cruiser, she received extensive modifications. Namely, in order to serve in a new role as a heavy fire support ship, her second main caliber turret was removed in favor of additional secondary weapons, support systems for her new engines and maneuvering jets needed for her now 3-dimensional environment, and perhaps most importantly, her new shields. Two new “wings” were added to accommodate the engines needed to propel her through space, which also carried new torpedo armaments to make up for the lost main turret.

Despite her age and the hasty redesign, the Lillaru served valiantly at the Battle of Aurora. Alongside her new big little sister, the Rillaru, the Lillaru would put up fearsome resistance. Despite only being reintroduced as a stopgap measure, the Lillaru proved more durable and more effective at suppressing other cruisers than the Aurorans had hoped. And as such, the Lillaru remains in service to this day.

This ship has been retrofitted and heavily modified to meet the specifications required by the Auroran Automata Expeditionary Division. It's equipped with the latest avionics and prototype upgrades, allowing it to outperform its predecessors while also a boasting lower sensor profile, thanks to the stealth technology behind its durable and unique AED coating.

The Lillaru has been one of the most reliable Yurian designs. Its age and longevity have been attributed to solid design choices of the Yurian Navy during the Unifying war, and is used to this day by the Auroran Navy… And, perhaps most unexpectedly, their enemies.

Shortly after the arrival of the United Auroran Federation within the Persean Sector, several skirmishes broke out between the expedition and the core world powers. Survivor tales were brutal, powerful warships fielding ancient ballistic weaponry, firing well beyond what most conventional weapons could in overwhelming numbers. Concerned about what could be a new pirate faction, the High Hegemon sent out a large task force to investigate the fringes. There they encountered a scouting party of the expedition, and before they could request identification, they were attacked. Unsurprisingly, the task force easily overwhelmed the small flotilla, and managed to capture one ship, a heavy cruiser, with her crew alive… for the most part.

Returning their prize to Chicomoztec, Hegemony agents interrogated her crew and learned the existence of the newly-arrived expedition fleet. Any more questions into the fleet’s purpose were met with vitriol and curses, the only thing they could gather was that these newcomers were here to enact vengeance against those who wronged them. In order to gather more intel on their new enemy, the High Hegemon had the cruiser, a Lillaru, refitted to match Hegemony standards and sent her to infiltrate Auroran ranks. As luck would have it, they encountered the Auroran leader, one Queen Aeria Charlotte Yuki, on their newly established foothold within the sector, New Aurora. No one knows what happened behind the meeting’s closed doors, save that it was heavily guarded. In the end, the Hegemony agents would return to the High Hegemon and report that the attack by the scouting fleet had been disavowed as an act of insubordination. Hegemon Daud disliked the thought of a power that could challenge the Hegemony, still he returned the modified cruiser as a “peace offering”. 

Federation high command knew this peace was shaky at best, and took the opportunity to study the modified cruiser to better understand this potential new threat. Auroran engineers found that little had been changed, save for the heavier armor and reinforced hull. While this slowed down the Lillaru, it made her much more durable, and far more of a persistent threat on the battlefield, especially combined with Auroran damage control practices.

Mary-Su- A one-of-a-kind modification for Admiral Mary Su Yi-Su's personal use based on her preferences, the ship was converted into a battlecarrier by opening the hull where its side missile mounts used to be. The single large mount and its internal mechanisms were removed in order to accommodate winged spacecraft and convert it into a hangar bay, with the magazine being converted into stowage for missiles and bombs for the ship’s new air wings. Additionally, a pair of heavy Reflecta-class external armor plates were added for greater protection of the flight deck from incoming attacks.

This ship has been modified differently compared to existing standards. She has been given the classification of 'Royal' type, and the third ship to receive this designation.

The United Aurora Federation prides itself in its own creations. While its enemies may have better technology or time-tested designs, it's extraordinarily rare when they decide to actively borrow from those beyond their borders. One notable exception took place shortly after the Battle of Aurora. All of the enemy ships destroyed were salvaged to fuel the now ever-hungry industries. However, a scouting party sent to the system’s edge found a cruiser unlike any of those that appeared during the Battle. Brought back for study, translators were able to make out her name on its ruined hull: Scholar.

With virtually all of her manufacturers busy building or designing new ships, and in dire need of a new mid-sized missile ship, the Federation would turn to its research division to study and reverse-engineer as much as they could before the expedition beyond the gate. This turned out to be far more costly and time consuming than anyone had expected, as many of the systems were too complex for current production facilities to handle. With time no longer on their side, the decision was made to scrap the more advanced systems and substitute them with currently available Auroran technology. This, ironically, ended up costing even more time, as the size of the much larger Auroran subsystems meant the interior of the ship had to be remodeled in order to accommodate them.

The research division would not finish the design for prototyping until well after the settling of New Aurora. The massive delay in the project caused many rumors to circulate regarding the cause. Perhaps the most outlandish of these was that the chief researcher had been locked up in the laboratory until the design process had been finished, only finishing it because the government threatened to cut funding for the project. Others claim that instead, the chief researcher went on a permanent vacation to Favonius after all the headaches of the pre-expedition problems came to a head. Regardless, the new ship, the Ularaya, would enter prototyping far too late to enter production, and would have been relegated to a museum ship until an enterprising captain came along and purchased her.

Despite looking nothing like her, the Ularaya is akin to a mini Purcellyra, sporting large numbers of missile launchers, including a scaled down version of her accelerated missile rack system. Unlike the Purcellyra, she can augment herself for other roles thanks to her hangars, equipping herself with anything from deadly bombers to nimble interceptors.

Upon their arrival in the Persean Sector, the United Aurora Federation found themselves with few local allies, either being openly hostile, or completely indifferent to their struggles against the local powerhouses. They did make one unlikely ally on the fringes of the Sector though: the Great Houses of Kassadar.

Unwilling to upset the balance of the Sector but still wishing to aid these new visitors, the Great Houses loaned some of their technologies to the Auroran engineers. This tech would be reported as stolen, and Auroran engineers would get to work reverse-engineering it for use aboard Auroran vessels. Unfortunately, due to the specific equipment needed to operate the machinery, no ship within the current arsenal or in its prototype stage could mount it. So they set about designing a new ship, one that was compatible with both the Houses’ High Domain advances and Auroran engineering protocols.

The result of this endeavor was the Procastria prototype cruiser. Thanks to her Kassadari technology, the Procastria was able to accelerate her own timeflow, greatly increasing her efficiency in combat, which to the naked eye made her far more nimble than she should be. However some sacrifices had to be made. While far more efficient for powering engines, the Melody power plant still struggles at producing enough power to support this time acceleration feature, making her time dilation less powerful than a proper Kassadari warship.

One of the cons of relying on ballistic weaponry is that ships may suddenly find themselves with enemies they are ill-equipped to face. The kinetic railcannons of the Tona-series fared poorly against heavily armored battleships, and the high-explosive power of the Herexa series anti-armor cannons did little more than cause minor ripples in shielding. The United Aurora Federation did not have a ship that could withstand the power draw of its experimental energy weapons yet, and so created the Maisya to support them while they solved the weapons’ overwhelming power consumption.

As a testing platform, the Maisya was not intended to see combat at all, and has very little armor whatsoever, so in order to withstand the rigors of depressurization, her hull was reinforced. She also had everything that the engineers thought would hypothetically increase flux draw, with the most powerful engines and shields available, and the most efficient Melody drive they could mount on a ship her size in order to support the unstable experimental energy weapons.

Due to being an Auroran weapons test platform, she would not enter production… at least officially. The Auroran Automata Expeditionary Division picked up the Maisya platform and gave her proper hull plating and crew accommodations. After stabilizing the energy weapons, creating the new Edge series flux-cannons and Merupaka point defense gun, the AED found that the engineers had accidentally created a very powerful warship. Her lack of armor and incredibly powerful engines, shields, and sensors, the Maisya became one of the most nimble warships available while still sporting heavy firepower. The Maisya would see use in special operations, her speed and agility allowing for lightning-fast guerilla tactics alongside the smaller ships in the Automata Expeditionary Division.

Auroran High Command has always been hesitant to make use of new frigate designs since the Nakiha’s introduction. With the Unifying War still fresh in mind, Yurian shipbuilders were reluctant to share the Nakiha’s plans with foreign manufacturers, and could not keep up the now global demand for new frigates. In response, Annabelle engineers introduced a new, cheaper, more easily produced frigate, the Lovela.

Many opponents of the Lovela were of the Federation’s Yurian dominant carrier-favoring admirals, the Permaisuri herself chief among them. They reasoned that many roles that a frigate can perform were more easily filled by the Federation’s fighter squadrons. Her supporters argued that this stance would void the Nakiha as well, and so the Lovela was reluctantly adopted as well. Annabelle shipwrights began production, and the Lovela’s low cost and manufacturing times, combined with Annabelle’s strong industrial base, eventually saw it outstripping the Nakiha in terms of population. When the expeditionary fleet made its way through the gate, Lovelas were among the first ships produced on the other side.

In spite of the initial misgivings, the Lovela’s induction into the fleet proved to be pivotal in the expedition's first years. Among the first opponents it faced were the Luddic Path’s swarm tactics. During the settlement of Favonius, the then-fledgling colony was attacked by a large number of Cerberus frigates. With few capital ships available and Nakiha patrols still en route, the only things standing between the colony and the swarm were the newly built Lovelas. Here it proved ideal, as its armor and firepower enabled it to outlast multiple Cerberus frigates while still being maneuverable enough to avoid torpedoes, despite being outnumbered 5 to 1. The defending Lovelas bought enough time for the defense fleet to organize and rout the enemy.

While still not as popular among frigate commanders as the Nakiha, the Lovela is regarded as legendary among the people of Favonius. Being the luxury hotspot of the UAF’s Persean home, this has led to a variety of local merchandise and tourist attractions based on them, the strangest of which being the first shipyard to produce them being called “The Gate of Heaven,” named as such after the release of the hit-song about the battle, “Flight of the Angels.”

Its two forward mounts are upgraded to composite mediums, further enhancing its forward firepower potential.

This ship was given up-to-date military-grade upgrades and a unique camouflage pattern belonging to the Auroran Royal Navy's November Fleet which is led personally by the Queen of Yuria herself. Additional armor plating and durable protective coating were installed to enhance the survivability of this ship; albeit at the cost of slightly lowered maneuverability and speed due to additional weight.

The Rama-class corvette is the smallest, fastest vessel designed for combat within the UAF’s fleet. Designed by Yuria to chase down Annabelle blockade runners, she finished development too late to play any significant role in the Unifying War. The finished plans for the Rama would remain shelved until the Expedition Fleet’s foray into the Persean Sector.

Prior to the introduction of the Rama to their shipyards, the United Auroran Federation believed that the Nakiha could handle just about any non-capital ship threat, as it had during the Battle of Aurora. However, on this side of the Gate, the Federation faced new opponents, ones who weren’t so reliant on larger vessels: pirates and the Luddic Path. Chief among the ships fielded by these factions were the nimble Cerberus frigate, ships capable of dancing around even the Nakiha. Annabelle engineers provided their own solution, the cheap and relatively heavily armored Lovela. However, while the Lovela could hold its own against the Cerberus, it lacked the required speed and agility to chase them down, meaning they were forced to remain close to the colonies they protected.

The revitalization of the Rama project began when AER-1A was searching for a better means to combat the frigate swarms when she encountered the plans in an old archive. Quickly assessing the vessel's strengths, AER-1A began deploying the Rama as a pursuit ship against the Cerberus frigate. Seeing its effectiveness in combat, Auroran High Command adopted the Rama, where it began serving as a substitute for fighter escort as well.

This ship has been retrofitted and heavily modified to meet the specifications required by the Auroran Automata Expeditionary Division. It's equipped with the latest avionics and prototype upgrades, allowing it to outperform its predecessors while also a boasting lower sensor profile, thanks to the stealth technology behind its durable and unique AED coating.

During the Battle of Aurora, the United Auroran Federation came face to face with a simple, effective cruiser design, which vastly outnumbered their own light cruisers and significantly more durable, making them one of the more pressing threats they faced. The Lillaru heavy cruiser could easily tear down other cruisers, although in the numbers faced, not fast enough, and the twin light cruisers Akatsuzuma and Inazuma simply did not have the endurance to fight these cruisers one on one, a difficult enough task with equal numbers, much less being outnumbered 2-to-1. The solution proposed by Auroran engineers was simple: a heavy cruiser with enough firepower and armor to make even a Rillaru captain nervous, the Seniraja.

The Seniraja was designed with one purpose and one purpose only: to engage and destroy other cruisers. Like many cruisers in the Auroran Navy, the Seniraja boasts higher than normal firepower, being more akin to a battlecruiser. Unlike the other cruisers, the Seniraja boasts enough armor to withstand attacks from multiple ships, earning it the nickname “The Pocket Battleship”. To drive this nickname further home, it shares many of the same strengths and weaknesses as the Reisen class battleships, that being the all-forward firing arcs of its weaponry, its slower movement compared to its more nimble counterparts, and the resulting vulnerability to flanking attacks. And just like the Reisen, in a 1-on-1 duel, the Seniraja is the king of its weight class. Sometimes when designing solutions, simplicity is best.

"Anyone could effectively command a Seniraja. They literally have one job: hug your fleet's flagship, point your bow at the closest enemy, and say 'weapons free'." - Alexandra Yamato.
