Now installed with an extra composite weapon mount in between the hulls, such minimal additional have increased the lethality of this already deadly cruiser.

This ship was given up-to-date military-grade upgrades and a unique camouflage pattern belonging to the Auroran Royal Navy's November Fleet which is led personally by the Queen of Yuria herself. Additional armor plating and durable protective coating were installed to enhance the survivability of this ship; albeit at the cost of slightly lowered maneuverability and speed due to additional weight.

While the Sunami retains the title of the United Auroran Federation’s best troop landing ship, it is seldom used, except in operations where success is paramount to the Federation’s strategic objectives. For any other operation, the Federation uses the much cheaper and easier to maintain Machi-Apa troop transport.

In the grand scale of galactic combat, it is often easier to simply deploy larger numbers of marines. While technically able to carry a greater number, the Sunami is a very expensive troop ship, essentially combining the facets of a long range support cruiser, an aircraft carrier, and a troop transport all in one. This results in both longer production times and much higher costs. The Machi-Apa, on the other hand, has one role and one role only: to deliver as many troops to the battlefield swiftly and safely, cutting down the need for specialized equipment. While not the most comfortable ship for its occupants, the sheer volume that may be deployed at any given moment makes them the go-to choice for large scale operations.

“This thing’s a piece of junk! There’s no way this’ll work!” “It’s either this, or a space-walk.” - Audio log from Berthing 2 aboard the UAFS Red Oak, prior to an assault on Entiaris.

After the Unifying War, the United Aurora Federation would begin its work on its space exploration vessels. Two were planned, the Cherry Vanguard and the Solvernia. Due to their sheer size, atmospheric construction was impossible, and the demand for raw material required far outstripped the ability for Aurora to handle, making it imperative the Federation had a reliable freighter for long distance hauling from neighboring planets. While the Nebula series blockade runners have served as the Federation’s primary cargo vessel, its size and armaments prevent it from being deployed en-masse. To remedy this situation, Vanguard Enterprises introduced the Junyo series  superfreighter.

The Junyo-Delta is a relatively simple design, simply taking a single extra-large cargo module and making it a ship of its own. Built out of the module itself, the Junyo-Delta is notably less appealing to the eye than other Auroran freighters. A portion of the module is set aside for crew living spaces and other vital machinery, and while these accommodations seem small on paper, the truth is that the allotted space is far greater than it seems. This proved to be a boon, as when pirates began attacking convoys, the Junyo-Delta saw its remaining living spaces converted into magazines and control systems for self-defence weapons. Opening up the interior of the remaining space also has made loading and unloading significantly easier, as well as allowing the Junyo to store cargo beyond its original dimensions, imitating her much larger predecessor. While this runs the risk the cargo may be damaged in a fight, its largely civilian usage means it stays away from the front, and seen as an acceptable risk.

After the Unifying War, the United Aurora Federation would begin its work on its space exploration vessels. Two were planned, the Cherry Vanguard and the Solvernia. Due to their sheer size, atmospheric construction was impossible, and the demand for raw material required far outstripped the ability for Aurora to handle, making it imperative the Federation had a reliable freighter for long distance hauling from neighboring planets. While the Nebula series blockade runners have served as the Federation’s primary cargo vessel, its size and armaments prevent it from being deployed en-masse. To remedy this situation, Vanguard Enterprises introduced the Junyo series  superfreighter.

Of course, with the large majority of these new superfreighters put into service to ferry large amounts of material for the Cherry Vanguard, Vanguard Enterprises used lessons from the Junyo-Delta to create the Miyo-Juliet. Like the other Vanguard freighters, the Miyo-Juliet comes with a modular cargo for ease of loading and unloading. Being scaled down for the planetside businesses of Aurora, the Miyo-Juliet started more akin to an airship than a spacecraft. The lack of pirates in-atmosphere has left her relatively unprotected compared to her larger counterpart, but she more than makes up for it by being not only more economical, but more space-efficient as well.

Vanguard Enterprises’ Supolev aerial hauler was a cargo airship that dominated the skies of Aurora, in use by many small businesses and moving companies. During the Unifying War, the Supolev became a very attractive target for all parties, so Vanguard armed it with their own anti-air missile platform. When time for the transition to the space-age came, the United Auroran Federation looked to Vanguard to create freighters. While the designs for the Junyo-Delta, Miyo-Juliet, and Cherry-Vanguard were being drafted, the Supolev received modifications to become space worthy as an interim freighter, becoming the Supolev-Alpha personal freighter.

The Supolev-Alpha would begin by ferrying personnel, tools, and equipment to new construction sites in orbit over Aurora. These would become orbital habitats and shipyards for the fledgling space fleet. As Miyo-Juliets and Junyo-Deltas started coming out, the Supolev-Alphas largely fell out of favor with large businesses and construction companies. Ironically, their small size proved to be a boon for the Supolevs, allowing them to escape the notice of pirates in search of the much more lucrative targets. Because of this, Supolevs in a transport fleet often had the role of carrying the most important cargo, their missile batteries being more than enough to handle pirate small-fry with nothing better to do.

Following the Battle of Aurora, a large amount of foreign wrecks and hazardous materials were left in orbit. Lacking experience in safely breaking apart ships, the United Aurora Federation turned to one of its foremost civilian experts in ship breaking and salvaging, the Nautical Preservation and Recovery Project. Having salvaged and reconstructed several warships from the ocean battlegrounds of the Unifying War, the Project would put together a team who would study the wrecks in space. This team would later grow into the new Salvage Categorization Institute. These SCI astronauts, or SCInauts for short, pioneered ship breaking in space for the Federation

In order to facilitate salvage in space, a new ship was constructed to suit their team’s needs. These large, cumbersome ships were ever-present in the months following the Battle of Aurora, scooping up everything they could find for identification and research. While a seemingly mundane and unimportant task, their swift and efficient cleanup of the space around Aurora allowed the Federation to quickly establish new orbital bases without fear of stray debris smashing into the stations. In their hurry to produce these ships, they were never given a name. However, with their widespread use and their almost exclusive ownership by the SCI, they would end up adopting the names of their crews, the Scinaut class salvage vessel.

Within the Persean Sector, Scinauts were the most important ships in the Auroran fleet. Without the ability to manufacture new ships, being able to salvage and recover damaged or destroyed ships became vital to the expedition’s survival. It was not until the settling of New Aurora that the Scinauts were allowed to rest, and even then, they remain ever-present in Auroran fleets, ready to pick apart and study the Federation’s adversaries in these unknown reaches of space.

The Auroran Navy has many old designs still in service. From the venerable Lillarus, to the swift Nebulas, but none are quite as respected as the Osela. Before the Unifying War began, the race to reach the stars had been the primary competition between the various nations. While Ceyae had been the first to space, and Annabelle took the feat of the first manned space flight, Yuria would be the first to truly send explorers to the depths of space. Osela and her crew would be absent for virtually the entirety of the Unifying War, and they would come back to a changed Aurora.

Designed under the watchful eye of Henrietta Charlotte, the Osela took flight carrying the hopes of the Yurian people, and the dreams of a young princess Aeria. Her powerful antennae allowed her to maintain contact with Yuria, until accidentally smashing it passing through an asteroid belt. Despite this she would continue to log her journey and record a wide variety of data, even though no one could see it until her return. This, coincidentally, was just as monumental as her departure: The entire world was watching as the Osela returned after her long voyage, entering the atmosphere mere minutes after the new Queen of Yuria, Aeria Charlotte, gave her speech marking the end of the Unifying War. 

While her return should have been the end of her service, the coming  expedition into the sector saw not only her return to service, but also become the name ship for a series of command vessels, the Osela Command Ship. If the Eyeseau served as the eyes and ears of the fleet, the Osela served as its brain. The powerful sensor array and navigational suite received software updates to interface with military systems, allowing her to effectively coordinate fleets, and her relatively small size meant she was much less of a target than the more immediate threats of the new United Aurora Federation’s capital ships. Additionally, her powerful communication array was reinforced to not only withstand asteroid impacts, but also the rigors of combat. The result was a powerful communication array that allowed for clear message traffic, even in the heat of battle under heavy jamming. While many questioned the decision of using an old exploration vessel as a command ship, none questioned its effectiveness. Its ability to hold a fleet together, along with Queen Aeria Charlotte’s fondness for the vessel, has earned it the nickname “The Queen’s Soul.”
An experimental strikecraft developed by the Nova Drive Yards R&D, fitted with unique melody engine reactor.

The Taufan II (NXP) is a Ceyaen next-generation upgrade package for the only multirole heavy fighter with strategic capabilities. Combat data delivered by a certain Vice Admiral and her test flight squadron revealed several key weaknesses and flaws of the original design. In order to address the issues and lessen the logistical strain, the legendary Minibreve cruise missile was exchanged in favor of the lighter Microbreve missiles mounted on special hardpoints on the wings.

Although it no longer possesses enough firepower to destroy a ship by itself, it's still enough to deter ships and even completely annihilate enemy wings with unforgiving salvo of tactical microbreve anti-air missiles.

These fighters were derived from the aging Super Aria and heavily modified as part of an aviation project commissioned to rival the Forlorn. The airframe was reinforced with experimental materials and the engines were retrofitted with a reverse engineered power plant of Persean origin. This newfound flexibility permitted the installation of energized autocannons and the addition of more wing pylons. The reduced power limitations also enabled the installation of Royal Maneuvering Jets, enabling skilled pilots to perform exceptional maneuvers.

Such upgrades proved to be highly effective, particularly in the hands of a pilot that understands both themselves and the fighter they pilot, further cementing the Hyper Aria’s position as the ultimate air superiority fighter.

Though the Hyper Aria exceeded all expectations, political and economic pressure saw the project canned at the prototype testing phase, and the few existing fighters were left to gather dust until the Tilted Dagger Incident.

A heavily modified Nekonyan craft whose alterations remain mostly classified. MIX is an acronym for Multi-dimensional Interceptor Experimental, a hypothetical upgrade package for the Nekonyan enabling a complete conversion into a fully automated stealth fighter. The new craft would be capable of rapidly traversing the battlefield with ease and striking decisively from unexpected angles.

Originally, a miniaturized Persean phase drive was to be installed and a Courtesan-class Neuko would serve as the pilot, a costly but necessary alternative as the risk proved to be too dangerous for a human pilot. Several daring experiments were conducted but the idea was eventually discarded as the Federation had a poor understanding of phase technologies at the time and was incapable of reproducing them. The adverse effects of P-space on AI was also discovered first-hand by the researchers who worked with the neuko pilots. 

Nevertheless, the remaining spacecraft that survived the experiments were given to an ace squadron and had their phase drive removed in exchange for external missile hardpoints made for brand new cluster kinetic cruise missiles.

Following the successful introduction of the Forlorn as the premier heavy fighter of the Federation, the latter quickly came to the dreadful realization that said fighter could barely keep up with many of the more advanced mass produced adversaries of the Persean Sector. The challenging demand for immediate modernization was accepted by a newcomer to the defense sector: High Beat Industries. A relatively small independent manufacturer specializing in avionics and electronics.

 Answering the demand for something packing a bigger punch while being able to shrug off incoming attacks, they transformed the craft from an armored fighter into an assault fighter capable of challenging significantly larger foes with its heavier armaments and missiles.

Regrettably, a prohibitive cost and a healthy dose of bureaucratic chaos led to limited deployment in the form of a single squadron. Ironic that the greatest adversary the project could not defeat came from within.

The Freesia-EX is an alternative air superiority fighter of Ceyaen design only recently built despite having long existed on paper. It inherits characteristics of past designs such as the Nekonyan and Super Aria and combines them to improve the strengths of both. Relying on heavy firepower and superior mobility to win fights while using its advanced control systems to keep enemies under constant pressure and unable to fight back. Despite being successful in its testing phases, the project was ultimately cancelled in favor of the Forlorn as the Federation’s mainstay heavy fighter. 

This fighter would have met the same fate as the Hyper Aria and other experimental projects had a wealthy benefactor not revived the project and overseen an exclusively limited production. Only those with the right connections can qualify to pilot these magnificent beasts.

The desire to redistribute the Taufan’s firepower as well as the need of a reliable platform capable of delivering its devastating payload to the enemy led to the creation of the Menascar II/N as an improvised solution. One capable of a swift and elegant delivery and return. The Taufan’s service record may be legendary, but many elements within Naval High Command believed that its reputation and the stories told by its survivors and witnesses overshadowed its true viability. They considered it an unacceptable risk to place so many resources in such a vulnerable fighter.

The Petunia is a brand new alternative to the Hyacinth and is the flagship product of the Automata Division’s Black Flower Project. It still resembles its predecessor: The Hyacinth, but now bears several notable differences. Instead of high explosive unguided bombs, it now carries a new breed of bombs capable of limited guidance armed with electromagnetic shaped warheads designed to effectively counter shields. This makes it ideal against adversaries that favor shields over armor. Like other Automata Strikecraft, it is designed to fly without human interference.

The Veronica is a next-generation anti-ship heavy fighter, designed, constructed and used exclusively by the Automata forces. It is heavily armed with energy autocannons and a single dual-barreled heavy turret mounted on top of the chassis capable of complete coverage. The size of this fighter deceives many of its opponents, who rarely expect it to be capable of flying swiftly enough to keep up with its smaller compatriots.

The Puterisaya is a sister variant of the Puterakamu typically only used by ships large enough to house and maintain this enormous fighter. An automated hunter killer capable of harassing ships much larger than itself and contesting comparatively large swathes of the battlefield if supported properly. Its vast array of powerful energy autocannons and anti-air missiles will ensure only few will survive its relentless strafing attacks provided it can be protected against enemy point defense.

As per request of the Automata Command, Puterakamu was recently upgraded with a new missile system capable of launching experimental cluster missiles. This powerful hunter-killer proved to be an invaluable addition to the Automata arsenal against the numerous threats that relied on sheer shield strength. It also comes equipped with high explosive anti-ship missiles to maintain its destructive power once the enemy’s shield goes down.

The middle child of the Black Flower series. The Alcea autonomous bomber carries a modest amount of Anti-Shield EMP bombs. It would also be the first bomber to be equipped with heavier caliber autocannon turrets loaded with kinetic rounds in place of the weaker and more common light turrets and medium turrets.

The smallest creation of the Black Flower Project. The Rosea is slightly nimbler than its conventional cousin and carries the smallest examples of the project's signature Discharge Blossom class bomb. This armament is an anti-shield EMP weapon capable of arcing energy through shields to damage the systems behind.

The Parang is a variant of the Keris class rocket attacker armed with energized kinetic rockets in place of high explosive rockets. It features the dark livery and autonomous combat capabilities standard for equipment developed and used by the Automata Expeditionary Division.

Many consider the Exoscar II to be a desperate attempt at modernizing the aging Exoscar so that its service may be extended for as long as possible. Despite numerous objections to the project and a collective desire to see the legendary bomber retired so that it may pass on gracefully, High Command maintained their stance. They insisted that a new set of torpedoes and the Automata Division’s specifications were all that was needed to rejuvenate this ancient chassis.

While the Super Aria’s reliability proved sufficient for the Navy’s conventional forces, the Automata Division believed that its human-centered specifications would result in wasted potential given that neukos do not suffer from the same limitations. This belief led to the development of the Nekonyan as a suitable choice for mass production. Boasting new energized armaments and anti-air missiles, It also comes with backwards compatibility for the few human pilots curious or capable enough to pilot it

The Revere is recognized as a member of the final generation of pure gunfighter craft alongside the Aria. Mostly ancient, its only new additions are its livery and autonomous flight modifications. Combat reports would demonstrate that even an obsolete platform such as this may still have a place on the battlefield given they still possess the capacity to escort larger ships.

The ultimate next-generation multirole heavy fighter designed by the Automata Expeditionary Division. This fighter’s specifications are so advanced that many human pilots need assistive programs to be able to operate it safely and effectively. Alternatively, a neuko can act as a suitable assistant and copilot. Its avionics and navigational systems grant it such maneuverability that it bears the nickname Dragon for the ferocity and speed with which it can dance around opponents.

The Hyacinth II/N eventually came to fruition following the decision to redistribute the Taufan’s strategic firepower. The navy needed a bomber capable of a larger or equivalent quantity of WMDs but wished to avoid sinking more of their already sparse resources into developing a new spacecraft. The bombs would be reinforced with hardened armor plating but remain unguided to save cost and space. Though its armament is now significantly more devastating, it still requires friendly spacecraft to saturate enemy defenses in order to cover these new munitions as they approach their target.

The Pengawas is commonly used for system patrol and escort duties for much larger ships. Equipped with defensive armaments such as flak cannons, wing mounted gatling cannon turrets and an array of anti-air missiles. Recently, she has also received a new addition to her arsenal: high-yield anti-air missiles. All of these defenses make the Pengawas ideal for protecting local airspace and intercept incoming threats through sheer ballistic firepower.

A large, experimental spacecraft whose strength is found in its powerful thrusters that enable a swift engagement and retreat upon payload delivery. This defining feature was once used for civilian use to transport cargo and personnel from surface to orbit as an SSTO ship before the Battle for Aurora. Now, it serves the Federation as a super heavy torpedo bomber, armed with torpedoes and kinetic rocket pods.

An extensive conversion of the Neoscar, the Menascar is an offensively geared gunship designed to strike deep and harass anything that dares fly too close to her whether ship, strikecraft or missile. Its originator’s powerful engines also allow her to redeploy with ease, though not to the same extent as other strikecraft. She also saw frequent deployment as close-air support for ground operations, providing much needed firepower for troops below.

The largest of the Flower series, the Hyacinth fulfills the same role as its relatives at a proportionally larger scale. Sacrificing quantity for quality, it now carries an enormous payload of armored unguided bombs. It is often used against large targets such as capital vessels, space stations or even ground targets provided a support carrier is able to retrieve it as its own engines are incapable of escaping the atmosphere of most worlds.

The Noversky has undergone a full modernization since its entry into naval service. The armaments aboard the older models proved to be ineffective against new enemies and so were replaced with more modern weaponry. Additionally, the airframe is now reinforced with modern materials and modular armor that was added to critical sections in order to improve survivability. It was given the nickname ‘Flapjack’ by pilots thanks to its unique shape.

The Sakurai is believed by many to be the most common bomber ever used by the Federation. Like its Flower siblings, it substitutes precision with volume. This classic saturation tactic proves to be surprisingly effective even in the age of spaceborne combat, as there are few evasive actions available to tightly grouped enemies or large targets in the face of such a large coverage.

The Hibiscus is the standard issue bomber of the Federation whose roots can be traced to before the Battle for Auroria. The smallest of the Flowers, It is commonly used in both space and ground operations. Its unguided bombs remain effective in space as it usually flies in large formations with others of its kind. Accuracy becomes a significantly lesser concern when enough of these bombs are deployed at once to mostly shield the crafts behind them from point defense.

Initially designed as a Single-Stage-To-Orbit (SSTO) spacecraft for civilian use, the Exoscar was militarized during the Battle for Aurora As the Federation desperately needed a ship capable of swiftly reaching orbital altitude to deliver a payload and return with minimal assistance. Though famed for its incredible speed, it came at a heavy fuel cost and the bomber was plagued with a short life expectancy. Despite this, many believed the Exoscar was simply too good to retire. Thus, the Federation never saw the need to develop a replacement and simply continued to upgrade and retrofit the old design.

The Keris is a defanged version of the obsolete ‘Minersky’ attacker. The removal of fixed armaments proved to be necessary in order to make room for disposable pods of high-explosive rockets. These are designed to be detachable so as to be swiftly disposed of once all rockets have been fired. These changes also indirectly enhance the spacecraft's maneuverability as it is much lighter without the large railgun and autocannon of its originator.

The Super Aria was originally designed to be used in-atmosphere on worlds like Old Auroria. However, the Battle for Aurora necessitated desperate measures as the Federation had not yet developed a dedicated fighter capable of exo-atmospheric flight. Upgrades and conversion kits were hastily developed to enable the Super Aria to fulfill this role. Initially, the glass canopy was still present but was swiftly replaced with a protective casing to improve pilot survivability. Maneuvering thrusters were installed at key points to take better advantage of space’s nature as a vacuum. Despite this, many strike craft designs still resemble aircraft. This is out of a desire to maintain atmospheric capabilities, to save costs and as a consequence of deeply ingrained design philosophies across the Federation’s defense industry and military.

An aging piece of Auroran engineering that remains in service despite predating the Battle of Auroria itself. This continued service is entirely attributable to its fairly simple design and ease of use. While the Federation now fields proper spacecraft for active combat, the Aria still sees action in back lines as training spacecraft and for patrol duties. Legend has it that if one masters the Aria, no creation of the Auroran people is beyond them.

The Forlorn is the Federation’s mainstay heavy fighter. They are used when smaller fighter wings can no longer reliably handle the density of a battle and were developed as the result of a joint fighter program between Yuria and Anabelle. Though new pilots consider it sluggish and heavy, its pair of heavy chainguns and autocannons as well as its AMSSM point-defense missiles make it a force to be respected. It was the sole victor of the mainstay heavy fighter contract, thanks to its relatively cheap assembly and maintenance cost as well as ease of use, with experience gained from Super Aria.

Rafflesya is the largest strikecraft fielded by the Federation. Though it is often seen paired with the Isenoria class Aviation Battleship, it predates the latter’s existence, which was modified during its development stages to accommodate the former. Capable of both atmospheric flight and spaceflight, the approach of this monstrous bomber signals an encroaching vaporization at the hands of its dreaded arsenal of guns and ballistic missiles. Though mitigated by its drones and PD coverage, its primary weakness is its lumbering pace which makes it an easy target, one of the several reasons it remains a rarity on the battlefield. Protect it well, and it shall sweep away your foes.

Though certainly effective, the original Rafflesya’s singular configuration left some wanting an alternative, thus leading to the creation of the Rafflesya II. The drone bays and internal missile bays were removed to make room for the more powerful systems needed for the Concord System to function. This also permitted the installation of more power-hungry weapons in place of the ballistic ordnance found on the original. Missile tubes were mounted on the outer hull as a substitute for the original ballistic missiles. The result trades its Alpha Strike potential for greater sustained fire capabilities

The second variant of the Moraffia was envisioned as an exploration vessel. As such, it was deemed necessary to provide it with a drone capable of protecting against fighters and missiles. The Ribut Protector is a small drone incapable of operating outside the immediate vicinity of its mothership. In exchange, it possesses explosive and energized autocannons as well as flak turrets. This as well as its ability to swiftly reposition itself and its surprising toughness make it ideal for defending against various threats.

Alongside the developing Drone Destroyer program came the desire to develop specialized autonomous drones capable of different roles in order to distinguish their parent motherships and justify their existence. Being little more than a pair of railguns with engines and flux coils, the Sokhuri specializes in long range harassment and lacks the ability to independently operate. As such, they are treated as more versatile turrets instead of proper strike craft. Many captains have been pleasantly surprised by their ability to maintain pressure on enemy shields and dissuade smaller ships from approaching.

The Minime is an ancient design dating back to the unification war era, being one of the earliest UAVs in Auroran history. They were used as cheap autonomous gunfighters and many pilots who fought against them found them irritating when alone and deadly when in groups. Following the Battle of Auroria, some military engineers saw them as potentially useful point defense drones if they could be modernized. With this in mind, they were provided with omnidirectional movement thrusters and were added to the arsenal of the Rafflesaya Strategic Bomber, one of the only strikecraft in the Auroran arsenal large enough to accommodate them but not so large as to render them redundant.
Solvernia's Phenex 608 railguns will each load three rounds of custom thermonuclear ordnance. Perfect against unshielded targets or for clearing a large area of massed spacecrafts.

text1:
The United Aurora Federation, commonly known as the UAF, is an organised, isolated and nominally neutral faction led by the popular monarch, the third Permaisuri of the Federation, Aeria Charlotte Yuki. It is a relatively new faction in the southwestern part of Persean Sector, with its history in the Sector extended only to a few cycles ago when its people were first known to the Core Worlds as “visitors from faraway places”. Standing strong with enough military power to tip the balance of power in the Sector, yet staunchly adherent to its unique peacefulness and isolation, the Federation is an enigmatic faction whose objectives and purposes remain obscure to many.

Compared to the common factions in the Sector, the Federation is uncharacteristically primitive, with its technological level being rated to be equivalent or less than the equivalent Sector’s Low-Tech and Midline technologies. Its unique doctrines and collection of ships distinctively reflected the traits of an early spacefaring civilization, a blend between conventional seafaring and modern spacefaring approaches. The distinct lack of phase technology and inexperience with the Sector technology are telling of the primitiveness, yet this technological gap is slowly being closed upon through the Federation’s curiosity, exploration, research and cooperation in the Sector.

Records remain sparse to this date, with the existing Domain records not mentioning anything in regards to the mysterious visitors. The Federation’s official claim is that they came from “an alternate dimension via an inter-dimensional wormhole”, specifically from “the mysterious Earth-like planet named Aurora”. The general public and major factions of the Core Worlds have largely dismissed this, calling it as “mere delusions” or “unrealistic propositions”. However, the fact that the Federation’s large expeditionary force appeared out of nowhere and potentially posed a threat to the established balance of power in the Sector could not be denied, even if they claimed to have visited “with no intention of hostility”. Perhaps for the best, for many cycles since settling in the far fringes, they have largely maintained its peaceful, isolationist stance and seem more interested in their own problems than the Sector.

Nevertheless, the ever enigmatic faction’s presence is increasingly harder to be ignored by the wider Persean Sector. Rumours persist that they are rejects who seek sanctuary faraway from the Core Worlds, fears spread of them preparing an invasion into the Core Worlds, curiosity stoked about the unheard peace and supposed new paradise there. Such baseless speculations are common for those who never set foot into the Federation before. One thing is clear however; they are outsiders in this foreign world and they will always be. The Aurorans’ gazes are always elsewhere, back to where they came from.

“However, we shall not give up! This faraway place is not where we shall be, nor belong to. We are mere visitors, those striving to seek answers for our homeland. Henceforth, I shall declare my promise to my fellow people, to those who remain faithful to our cause, to our ownself! I swear upon the Dove’s Wings, the wings which carried us through the dark days, that we will find our way home! It will be a great undertaking, a perilous journey, but I ask for your unwavering faith in me, that we will leave no one behind and together, we will see our home again. May the heaven bless and keep us all.”
– Permaisuri Aeria Charlotte Yuki III, excerpt from “The Queen’s Crossing Speech”

The United Auroran Federation’s home away from home. The Federation was originally supposed to establish its Persean foothold on Favonius. However, the abrupt closure of the dimensional gate left them stranded, and the relatively small land masses of Favonius proved to be limiting for sustainability for the fleet. Thankfully, a Straffia drone had come across a habitable world almost identical to Aurora in a nearby solar system. The Federation would opt to settle here, christening the planet New Aurora. While a bit lacking creative naming, the Federation has nevertheless transformed the planet into the spitting image of its namesake. Its distance from the core worlds means it is largely untouched by the chaotic power struggles of Persean natives, meaning security details have largely only needed to contend with Pathers, pirates, and the occasional AI menace. With no major threats, the population has exploded in recent years thanks to a massive influx of refugees from the core worlds in hopes of better living conditions. The increase in manpower has allowed for the construction of skyscrapers, and most recently, the Monarch Complex, the Federation’s seat of power and home to the Permaisuri within the Persean Sector. All of which are under the watchful eye of the Permaisuri's own Royal November Fleet.

New Aurora’s sole moon. Despite its small size, Lunamun is of vital strategic importance. A large portion of the Federation’s vital infrastructure is spaced out in underground facilities, including, but not limited to: hidden hangar facilities for secure and safe launch of smaller ships of the Federation, the heart of the Neuko hivemind, and the long-range deep space intelligence network. Above the surface, the moon also hosts the Federation’s orbital shipyards and functions as the primary staging points for fleets both from New Aurora and Lunamun itself. Only a handful of facilities remain exposed on the surface, and even then, most reside in lunar craters, with the natural shielding of the crater walls allowing for focused shielding against attacks from above.

Entiaris was one of the first attempted colonies of the United Auroran Federation. In need of raw materials needed to kickstart their manufacturing infrastructure after the closure of the dimensional gate, the UAF set up a mining outpost on the planet. Unfortunately, the Federation’s dire lack of manpower led to a catastrophe resulting in the loss of the colony. For starters, the lack of personnel needed to vet the tide of refugees meant a large number of the core worlds’ unsavory characters were able to infiltrate the colony. Additionally, a divide was forming between the refugees and the Federation, as only Federation officers were left in charge, and refugees were left in conditions not much better than what they fled. Eventually, backed by the pirates of Kanta, the miners revolted, and without the personnel to control the riots, the UAF was forced to abandon the colony. Pirates were more than happy to take over the abandoned outpost, becoming the headquarters of the pirates operating within Federation space, digging in. Kanta’s support made it virtually impossible for the still undermanned Federation to eradicate them entirely. The UAF would instead move its mining industry to Nur, using the lessons learned from Entiaris to avoid yet another collapse of authority.

The inter-dimensional choco lava is one of the special commodities commonly found in New Aurora. The sauce is made from a processed kokoa and other ingredients, then packaged in boxes ready to use and delivery. According to some of the locals, they often get nostalgic flashbacks about their homeworld and the good memories when they ate it. Now, it's just memories of a thing long past, especially with their home now impossible to reach.

The Auroran bakeries are famously known for their delicious pastries. Donuts are one of many choices a hungry person may enjoy during their visit in New Aurora. Various toppings and flavorings are used for different types, but sugar and chocolate combo are definitely one of the local favorites. These pastries are a cultural thing to the Aurorans, some may be born into the Sector and not know how to imagine how their home might look like. At least they can use their imagination after they get a taste of it.

An army cannot work with their stomachs empty, especially with home sickness in mind. The Permaisuri are aware of this potential risk and invested funds into the local food industry, encouraging them to produce them by giving subsidies and special economical benefits. This one is filled with delicious yet luxurious combo of croissants and pretzels, along with complimentary candies and a lolipop. One would always see a cute cartoonistic rendition of Aeria's smile on the box cover along with description of how beautiful and lovely their world was like on the other side. At the end of the paragraph, there will always be encouraging words to never give up as hope is one what make Aurorans different to any other.

This diced and dried goods are made from processed pineapple fruit, usually found in tropical planets, coated with a little bit of sugars before being packaged into bags of pineapple goods. This food is good for snacking while giving a comparable nutritional value for space-faring civilization, the diced pineapple being dried also helped with preserving the food itself.

Vanilla ice cream is a sweet frozen dessert made from milk, cream, sugar, salt and flavored from a combination of various pure vanilla extract. Its wonderful taste are also refined and mixed alongside other dessert products as well. This particular ice cream was named after the queen after she announced the official opening of the Favonius Resort World to the public, an important place where people can temporarily forget and enjoy life for just a little bit longer.. if they survive the initial queue struggle of course.
text3: uaf_vanilla_ice_cream

text1: The BFB-90/XL "AWP Class 1" is an experimental super large-yield bomb, developed and used in an orbital spacecraft known as the Rafflesya. it was said to be the most powerful non-WMD weapon to ever be developed in the Auroran arsenal. The bomb is primarily an air burst bomb intended for anti-station and capital purposes but it can also be used in atmospheric condition as well. Its extremely volatile warhead will basically blanket a large area with atomic-like fire and annihilate everything in its wake.

Typically categorized as "atmospheric munitions", these specialized ammunition are reinforced with heat resistant coating and plating in order to increase their chance to penetrate into atmosphere without disintegrating. This comes with several downsides such as higher cost and requiring special facility for production. Depending on the size, some may have lesser explosive filler in order to make room for necessary upgrades. Auroran naval guns are already readily made with these specialized rounds in mind and doesn't require a dock visit for upgrade.

The Crown Princess model of Neuko core is classified as an Alpha level AI. Originally built for entertaining people, the Crown Princess has a higher memory capacity, allowing it to “remember” customers as well as their tastes. The Federation used this trait in order to create an artificial officer that meshes well with the fleet.

As her name implies, the Crown Princess is the crown jewel of an Automata fleet. With the most powerful CPUs available, Crown Princesses can run the most algorithms and protocols at once, offering unmatched flexibility. The Crown Princess’s data web is powerful enough to coordinate the entire fleet, typically from the dominating position of the Federation's capital ships. In addition to her own data web, the Crown Princess also maintains a direct link with the Neuko Collective, allowing her to draw on tactics and information available to other command models, even those not in the battlespace.

Selling AI cores on the open market is prohibited by most factions. However, higher-up faction representatives are often willing to exchange AI cores for large sums of cash when communicated with directly, though their reasons for doing so may vary.

The Princess model of Neuko core is classified as a Beta level AI. Originally built for entertaining people, the Princess has a higher memory capacity, allowing it to “remember” customers as well as their tastes. The Federation used this trait in order to create an artificial officer that meshes well with the fleet. 

Compared to the Courtesan, the Princess has a stronger CPU, allowing for additional protocols and algorithms to be run simultaneously. The Princess typically can be found in control of mid-size ships like cruisers, and is able to coordinate a small battlegroup, being able to interface with both its ship and any other Courtesans it shares its data web with. The result is that the Princess has heightened battlefield awareness, able to monitor the battlespace through the eyes of its subordinates. The Princesses only answer to their commanders or any Crown Princesses in the fleet.

Selling AI cores on the open market is prohibited by most factions. However, higher-up faction representatives are often willing to exchange AI cores for large sums of cash when communicated with directly, though their reasons for doing so may vary.

The Courtesan model of Neuko core is classified as a Gamma level AI. Originally built for entertaining people, the Courtesan has a higher memory capacity, allowing it to “remember” customers as well as their tastes. The Federation used this trait in order to create an artificial officer that meshes well with the fleet. 

Courtesan’s will strictly follow their commander’s battle doctrine, while maintaining enough memory of other officers in the fleet in order to coordinate effectively in large group environments. However, they are limited by their CPU size, meaning they cannot load nearly as many protocols and algorithms as other command models can, therefore these must be set well in advance. Courtesans are often controlling smaller ships, but lack the ability to control anything more than their own ship, instead answering to Princess and Crown Princess command models.

Selling AI cores on the open market is prohibited by most factions. However, higher-up faction representatives are often willing to exchange AI cores for large sums of cash when communicated with directly, though their reasons for doing so may vary.

A Domain-era space station loosely resembling a centuries-outdated O'Neill cylinder design, abandoned long before the UAF's arrival to the Sector. It was deemed irreparable by many, but someone determined enough could take advantage of its vast storage potential.

An enormous space station which can host shipyards, habitats, factories, or large stockpiles of weapons and supplies. These platforms allow humanity to project military and industrial power throughout known space.
A wreckage and remains of an Auroran fleet, it wails a corrupted emergency beacon again and again.

Following their arrival in the Persean Sector, Favonius was one of the first planets the United Auroran Federation stumbled upon, and the first colony to be set up. An extreme stroke of luck, as the planet was almost a perfect paradise. The near permanent summer and the tropical weather made a relaxing environment for anyone after a long voyage. However, as a water planet, there was not a lot of buildable land as virtually all of it were islands of varying sizes., and the water got significantly deeper the further from shore you went, meaning that underwater facilities were out of the question. Thankfully, the nearby system had a habitable planet with much larger land masses as well, which would become New Aurora. With the need to establish a permanent base for the fleet out of the way, the Federation decided that they could use Favonius as a recreational facility for their tired troops, and potentially make money off of any visitiors.
A station for directly managing the Favonius, also a place to park spaceships while visiting the world. Direct access to Favonius bypassing this station is illegal.

Primarily composed of hydrogen and helium gases. The temperature and pressure at the core are extreme, but not enough to sustain the deuterium fusion that would turn the planet into a brown dwarf star. This particular giant has several compounds used in the production of drugs. This was found out completely by accident when a pirate freighter flew a little too close to the atmosphere after skirmishes with Auroran authorities severely damaged its engines. The byproduct of the biological matter mixing with the composition of the gas is what produced the requisite materials for drug synthesis. A single person is hardly enough to produce anything, so the pirates usually capture a large number of prisoners. Roughly around 10,000 are sacrificed in mass rituals to permeate the atmosphere with drugs.

Favonius, being the heart of UAF civilian industry and an almost perfect habitable planet, was envied by all factions. As a result, all major players in the Sector had a vested interest in acquiring it for their faction. However, clashes between “diplomats” and “envoys” eventually spiraled out of control, and Favonian space was swallowed in turmoil as fleets battled it out for seats at the negotiation table with the UAF. Collateral damage to the planet finally caused the UAF to step in and cease all diplomatic communications with outsiders, and the relatively lightly armed cruise ship fleets were scattered by the Federation navy. While the space above has long since been cleared, the debris from what is now called the Tourism War can still be found in the oceans of Favonius. The names etched into the memorial here belong to the people who helped clean up and rebuild in the aftermath.

The Keycard Exchange Management now offers special bundles at a special exchange rate. Each bundle is tailored for various starfarers’ needs in exploring the Sector, from obtaining miscellaneous supplies to procuring new ships with only a single exchange.

Favonius is home to many Auroran reserve officers and independent contractors, some are open to joining promising starfarers in their adventures. Adhering to the exchange rate and contracts as outlined by the Auroran Administrative Bureau are all it takes to recruit new officers within one’s ranks. Auroran’s own Neuko AI Cores are also available for exchange as part of the officer exchange programme.

The Auroran Naval Research Bureau has since released some of its prototype and unique designs for limited field testing and combat operations. Limited procurement of these unique ships are open for friendly starfarers who wish to obtain the latest capabilities or interested in ships normally unavailable in the regular markets.

Unique demands and the constant need to catch up in the Sector’s arms race sees the Auroran Naval Research Bureau constantly develop new armaments and improve on existing ones to better equip the fleets in dealing with future threats. Some of the prototype and specialist designs find their ways into the Exchange List for field testing or use with other prototype ships.

Colony development is always an expensive endeavour for independent starfarers, hence the Auroran Administrative Bureau has generously approved economic assistance plans in the form of special colony items. Various independently developed colony items that drove Auroran’s economy and administration are available for exchange and provide similar benefits to colony administration and economy to that of Domain’s colony artefacts.

The Keycard Exchange Management offers supplies and other miscellaneous items for exchange as well. The most notable exchanges for trusted starfarers however are the blueprint access for various Auroran vessels and munitions for planetary operations.
The Favonius Economic Trade Zone Management welcomes you. Enjoy your stay and feel free to browse our wares!

The Federation Navy is rather famous for its multiple ace fighter programmes, with the Auroran Naval Research Bureau continuously upgrading existing strikecraft designs with experimental features and, if funding allowed, designing entirely new strikecrafts. Due to popular requests, the ANRB decided to release certain experimental strikecrafts into the Exchange List.

The Federation values survey data greatly as part of its strategic needs for safe navigation, information gathering, resource gathering and supporting materials for friendly private ventures. The Keycard Exchange Management accepts the widely used data as organised in the traditional Domain Colonial Ministry’s guideline in exchange for Confectionary Coins.

The Federation has been running a campaign of recovering lost Access Keycards since the Crossing in exchange for credits and recently here, Confectionery Coins. Multi-layered encryption and killswitch in the event of hacking or reverse-engineering have made their values particularly fickle in the general market of the Sector. However, for the Aurorans, various Access Keycards represent lost information that could be useful. Exchange rates of all Access Keycards will be based on their respective Tier Colour.
A cache of supplies, equipment and other logistical materials, drifting aimlessly and waiting for its eventual recovery. Signals emitted confirm it belonged to UAF.
A cache of supplies, equipment and other logistical materials, drifting aimlessly and waiting for its eventual recovery. Signals emitted confirm it belonged to UAF.

Vermillion Station, while managed and built by the UAF, is a neutral zone. All ships on official business to New Aurora must first visit to register here. While nothing stops vessels from traveling straight to New Aurora, without the permits issued here, business is largely restricted to purely civilian trade markets. Most people visiting the system will inevitably end up at Vermillion, due to its close proximity to the system’s inner hyperspace jump point. As the gateway into Auroran territory, Vermillion is perhaps the most familiar locale in the system, being closer to your standard Persean Sector station than the tidiness of most Auroran ones.

The industrial base of the United Auroran Federation, Nur supplies a vast majority of the raw material for the UAF. Having lost the colony at Entiaris, the Federation needed to re-establish its mining industry in order to keep its shipyards operational. With trust already damaged with the refugees from the core worlds, and the personnel shortage still in effect, the Federation would take a different approach for policing the new colony: hiring mercenaries. Initially, they were reluctant to trust independent contractors with security, especially those who worked for the highest bidder.  Despite this, it was more important to restart mining before material deficiencies began to hamper the fleets. As a result, Federation high command decided to employ personnel well versed in back alley dealings and underworld treaties in order to control them. This also helped smooth out some piracy issues, as black market dealing would be overlooked, so long as no trouble was made. While these policies did help revitalize the mining industry, Nur has become a sort of black sheep within the Federation as a result of these "sanctioned" dealings.

The Nova Drive Yards are some of the largest orbital facilities built in recent history. Built to produce the massive super-capitals of the United Auroran Federation, the primary station dwarfs most orbital fortresses. It had been considered to solely use the Cherry Vanguard to supply and maintain the fleet. As large as it was, the Cherry Vanguard could not carry enough material and personnel to support itself and Solvernia for a long period of time. The UAF decided to deploy the Nova Drive Yards on the other side of the gate, which helped alleviate a multitude of issues. First and foremost was the maintenance of the Expeditionary Fleet’s guardian Solvernia. Second, to function as a mobile base for the Expeditionary Fleet while exploring the Persean Sector. And finally, after extensive renovation of the primary station, an impressive orbital defense platform for the initial stages of the expedition and colonization beyond the gate until permanent, dedicated facilities could be set up.

The Ganchab Listening Post originated as the Federation’s independent hub with fueling operations planned as a secondary function before the construction of Vermilion Station rendered the partially-finished station redundant. To cover the cost and funding other more pressing constructions, the station was eventually put on sale for interested parties, with the Tri-Tachyon Corporation showing the most interest due to the Neuko technology and rumors of defected Tri-Tachyon scientists. Through a series of negotiations and aggressive bidding, the corporation managed to gain complete control of the station and established an intelligence post close to Federation. The corporation hopes to keep an eye on the Federation’s technological developments and economic developments through the post, fearing that it could be a business rival in the future. However, the Tri-Tachyon’s board of directors was keener on using the station more than just a spying station. The original cover of it being a mere company branch is thus taken literally, expanding into a more profitable trade, research and smuggling station.

Los Scrapyard is dominated by a group of pirates called the Los Scrappers, salvagers turned pirates after being backstabbed by one too many clients. Taking out slights and transgressions on corporate traders was only natural. After attempts by various interest groups to assert influence on the scrapyard through subterfuge and assassination, the rest of the sector, save for Kanta's clan, earned a black mark from the scrapyard's inhabitants. The Auroran supply ships are an all too tempting prize, whipping the pirates into a frenzy. Scrappers are filled with wonder, fantasizing about what exotic goods the Aurorans have, and they are all too eager to venture into Auroran space to find out for themselves.

Clotho serves as a convenient resource point for the Tri-Tachyon Corporation based in Ganchab, with mining operations occasionally undertaken to supplement the incomes. At least on paper. It is not uncommon to see armed ships marked with Tri-Tachyon emblems linger around the surface, longer than necessary for a minor mining operation. The unusual cautiousness leads to some speculation of Tri-Tacyhon secret bases and research facilities being constructed underneath Clotho’s icy surface. Tri-Tachyon themselves publicly denied these rumors, though no one can verify whether the corporation is just mining peacefully or doing more than just preventing unwanted guests in the area.

The ice from Atropos is the most direct water source for Los Scrapyard. It is common to see ice merchants take their ships down with a handful of crews to harvest ice blocks to sell as water, sometimes melting them via the heat of their own engines. Though ice harvesting can be a lucrative career, water cartels on Los Scrapyard are said to be very territorial of the softest ice fields. Expect lethal consequences when found harvesting in a cartel's territory without permission.

Lanun Listening Post has had various roles over the years. Mining station. Waystation. Hegemony Listening Post. When the Hegemony military abandoned this place, Ludd's message thrived in the squalor. It didn't take long for many on Lanun to adopt the Luddic Path's ways, their newfound faith helping them cope with their destitute lifestyle. Lanun is no friend to Aurorans. Children here are taught to throw garbage or feces at images of Neukos. A common story is that the cold metal witches will arrive wordlessly to whisk souls away. Whether this is based on any true story is unknown. The people of Lanun, however, believe it to be all too real.
